target.title: fix(deepinfra): load all DeepInfra models when user wants to browse t…
target.body: # fix(deepinfra): load all DeepInfra models when user wants to browse them during onboarding ## Summary - **Problem**: DeepInfra plugin pinned the chat catalog to 7 hardcoded manifest entries (a wizard-vs-discovery guard short-circuited discovery once the auth wizard seeded models), had no live discovery seam for image-gen / video-gen, and silently dropped Anthropic `cache_control` markers when proxying `anthropic/*` models — prompt caching was advertised eligible but never fired. - **Solution**: Wire DeepInfra's `/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta` (the agent-tagged projection now shipping in the backend) through a per-surface helper that buckets responses by short-alias tag (`chat`/`vlm`/`embed`/`image-gen`/`video-gen`/`tts`/`stt`). Register live image-gen and video-gen catalogs via `registerModelCatalogProvider` (OpenRouter pattern). Replace the OpenRouter-only Anthropic cache wrapper with a deepinfra-local one gated on the model id. - **What changed**: new `surface-model-catalogs.ts` (live image-gen + video-gen catalog providers + `resolveModelCapabilities`); new `cache-wrapper.ts` (Anthropic ephemeral cache marker injection for `anthropic/*` on DeepInfra); refactored `provider-models.ts` (per-surface bucketed discovery, API-key gated, 5-min cache, sync static fallback); media-provider builders now accept the per-surface catalog; `index.ts` feeds all media surfaces from one source + registers the new catalog providers; `media-models.ts` `DEFAULT_*` constants demoted to `*_FALLBACK_MODELS`; TTS default bumped to `Qwen/Qwen3-TTS`. - **Scope boundary**: No edits outside `extensions/deepinfra/`. No SDK changes (`src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts` is byte-identical to `main`). TTS/STT/VLM/embed surfaces still use static fallback only — extending `UnifiedModelCatalogKind` to cover them is a separate SDK PR. ## Motivation DeepInfra ships hundreds of models, but openclaw only surfaced 7 hardcoded chat IDs — every new model needed a code change here. The DeepInfra backend now emits surface-tagged responses on `/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta`, so the plugin can map tags → surfaces dynamically. Anthropic prompt caching on DeepInfra was also silently broken: the runner advertised eligibility but the OpenRouter-only stream wrapper short-circuited on `provider !== "openrouter"`, so `cache_control` markers never went out. ## Change Type - [x] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [x] Refactor required for the fix ## Scope - [x] Integrations ## Linked Issue/PR - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Real behavior proof ### Environment Hosted Lightsail VM `ip-172-26-8-116` (3.71.115.124), Ubuntu 24.04, Node 24.15.0, openclaw 2026.5.19, branch `ed9acb0519`. Live network to `api.deepinfra.com`. DeepInfra key configured via auth-profile store + `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` env (both paths exercised). `openclaw.service` live state: ``` Active: active (running) Memory: 582.9M (max 3.5G) Python helper sidecar: PID 1388 (fs-safe pinned writes enabled) ``` - **Steps after this patch**: ```bash pnpm build pnpm test extensions/deepinfra rm -rf ~/.openclaw-dev DEEPINFRA_API_KEY= pnpm dev --dev capability model providers | grep deepinfra DEEPINFRA_API_KEY= pnpm dev --dev capability model list | grep '"provider":"deepinfra"' | wc -l DEEPINFRA_API_KEY= pnpm dev --dev capability image providers | grep deepinfra DEEPINFRA_API_KEY= pnpm dev --dev capability audio providers | grep deepinfra ``` - **Evidence after fix**: - `capability model providers` → `{"provider":"deepinfra","count":88,"defaults":["anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5","anthropic/claude-opus-4-7","anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"],"available":true,...}` (was: 7 on `main`). - `capability model list | grep '"provider":"deepinfra"' | wc -l` → 88 (was: 7). - `capability image providers` → deepinfra row with `FLUX-1-schnell` default + fallback allowlist. - `capability audio providers` → `{"id":"deepinfra","configured":true,"capabilities":["image","audio"],"defaultModels":{"image":"moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5","audio":"openai/whisper-large-v3-turbo"}}`. - `pnpm test extensions/deepinfra` → 59/59. - `pnpm check:changed` → exit 0 (one pre-existing unrelated lint failure in `scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs:398`, last touched in `85f9276624`). - **Not tested**: end-to-end TTS/STT audio round-trip and a real image/video-gen request against the live backend. The runtime providers register correctly and discovery returns the right shape; downstream inference was not exercised. ## Root Cause - (a) `augmentModelCatalog` short-circuited with `existingDeepInfraCount > DEEPINFRA_MODEL_CATALOG.length`, skipping discovery whenever the auth wizard had seeded entries first. - (b) Media surfaces had no `registerModelCatalogProvider` wiring. - (c) `wrapStreamFn` reused `createOpenRouterSystemCacheWrapper`, which short-circuits on `provider !== "openrouter"`, so Anthropic ephemeral cache markers never reached the payload for DeepInfra-routed Anthropic models even though `isCacheTtlEligible` returned `true`. **Missing guardrail**: no test asserted that `wrapStreamFn` applied `cache_control` markers for `anthropic/*` ids; no test asserted that the augmented catalog grew past the manifest size on successful discovery. ## Regression Test Plan - **Coverage level**: [x] Unit test [x] Seam / integration test - **Target tests**: - `extensions/deepinfra/cache-wrapper.test.ts` (new) — asserts `cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" }` markers are applied for `anthropic/*` ids and skipped otherwise. - `extensions/deepinfra/surface-model-catalogs.test.ts` (new) — asserts the live catalog providers return `null` without a key, return `null` on empty surface buckets, and project entries with the correct shape otherwise. - `extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts` (extended) — per-surface bucketing + manifest fallback path. - `extensions/deepinfra/index.test.ts` (extended) — augmentModelCatalog merges discovered + wizard-seeded entries instead of suppressing one. - extensions/deepinfra/cache-wrapper.test.ts (4 tests) exercises createDeepInfraAnthropicCacheWrapper against anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 & anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5 and asserts cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" } is injected into the system message under both string-content and array-content shapes; negative test confirms non-anthropic ids pass through untouched. - extensions/deepinfra/provider-models.test.ts (3 new cases) mocks openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-auth.isProviderApiKeyConfigured and asserts hasDeepInfraApiKey({ env: {}, agentDir }) falls back to the auth-profile store with the correct provider/agentDir args, short-circuits on env var, and returns false when both env and store are empty. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - **Chat catalogue**: with `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` set, OpenClaw surfaces the full DeepInfra catalogue (88 chat + 38 vlm today across `anthropic/`, `ByteDance/`, `deepseek-ai/`, `google/`, `meta-llama/`, `Qwen/`, `zai-org/`, …). Without a key, the curated 7-model manifest fallback is served. - **Image-gen + video-gen**: a live `registerModelCatalogProvider` now refreshes per-kind catalogs from DeepInfra when a key is configured. Without a key, the curated static fallback is served. - **TTS default**: `hexgrad/Kokoro-82M` → `Qwen/Qwen3-TTS`. Users on the implicit default will now hit Qwen3-TTS; Kokoro stays in the fallback list and can be selected explicitly. - **Onboarding picker**: discovered models are no longer hidden behind wizard-seeded entries. - **Anthropic prompt caching** actually fires on DeepInfra-routed `anthropic/*` requests. Multi-turn conversations should show non-zero `cache_creation_input_tokens` on turn 1 and `cache_read_input_tokens` on turn 2+. - Backward compatible; no config/env/migration changes. ## Diagram ```text Before: [user pastes API key] -> [auth wizard seeds 7 manifest entries] -> [augmentModelCatalog: count > MODEL_CATALOG.length? -> skip discovery] -> [picker shows 7 models] [anthropic/* on DeepInfra request] -> [isCacheTtlEligible -> true] -> [createOpenRouterSystemCacheWrapper -> provider !== "openrouter" -> short-circuit] -> [payload sent WITHOUT cache_control markers, prompt cache never fires] After: [user pastes API key] -> [auth wizard seeds entries] -> [augmentModelCatalog: always run discovery] -> [discoverDeepInfraSurfaces: GET /v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta] -> [bucket by surface alias tag] -> [picker shows 88 chat + 38 vlm + ...] [anthropic/* on DeepInfra request] -> [isCacheTtlEligible -> true] -> [createDeepInfraAnthropicCacheWrapper: model.id starts with "anthropic/" -> apply patch] -> [payload sent WITH cache_control: {type: "ephemeral"}, prompt cache fires] ``` ## Security Impact - New permissions/capabilities? **No** - Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** - New/changed network calls? **Yes** — one read-only GET to `https://api.deepinfra.com/v1/openai/models?sort_by=openclaw&filter=with_meta` per process per 5 minutes, gated on `DEEPINFRA_API_KEY` being configured. The endpoint is unauthenticated; the gate is a UX choice (pre-auth flows show the static fallback, no useless network traffic). No PII or secrets in the request. - Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** - Data access scope changed? **No** ## Human Verification (Observed Result) ran `pnpm dev --dev onboard` with provider deepinfra and successfully loaded all discovery models: Tested various `pnpm dev --dev capability ` commands (see section "Real behavior proof") Tested TTS in the UI dashboard: ## Risks and Mitigations - **Risk**: production DeepInfra backend hasn't (yet) tagged any `video-gen` model with the OPENAI tag, so the `video-gen` surface bucket from live discovery is empty. **Mitigation**: the live catalog seam returns `null` on empty buckets so the static `DEEPINFRA_VIDEO_FALLBACK_MODELS` list wins. Once the backend tags T2V models, the live catalog takes over automatically — no deepclaw redeploy needed. - **Risk**: TTS default change (`Kokoro-82M` → `Qwen3-TTS`) could surprise users on the implicit default. **Mitigation**: Kokoro stays in the fallback list and can be selected explicitly; documented in CHANGELOG.

target.title: refactor(agents): carry resolved model runtime through auth
target.body:
Opened on behalf of Onur Solmaz (@osolmaz). Work in progress draft PR.

AI-assisted implementation requested by a maintainer.

## Summary

Carry the resolved model runtime through the embedded agent path.

This keeps the selected provider, the final model API/base URL, the provider refs that own auth/stream hooks, and the runtime plan together after model resolution. Later auth and stream setup use those resolved facts instead of guessing from the provider id again.

Refs #80487.

## What Changed

- Added a resolved model runtime descriptor with provider/model ref, effective transport, auth provider refs, and source metadata.
- Updated sync and async model resolution to return that descriptor alongside the existing resolved model.
- Passed resolved runtime auth facts into embedded-run credential resolution, synthetic provider auth, and synthetic-profile defer hooks.
- Let provider runtime auth hooks use explicit resolved provider refs when available.
- Built the agent runtime plan from the resolved descriptor after runtime model updates.
- Routed stream hook lookup through the selected model API owner when a custom provider's selected model changes API, and made Ollama selected-model `baseUrl` override provider `baseUrl`.

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts extensions/ollama/index.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/auth-controller.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.synthetic-auth-discovery.test.ts` - passed, 10 files / 402 tests.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo` - passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` - passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo` - passed.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo` - passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts extensions/ollama/index.ts extensions/ollama/provider-discovery.ts extensions/ollama/src/discovery-shared.ts extensions/ollama/index.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/auth-controller.ts src/agents/resolved-model-runtime.types.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/resolved-model-runtime.ts src/plugins/types.ts src/plugins/provider-external-auth.types.ts` - passed.
- `git diff --check` - passed.
- `node --import tsx scripts/check-import-cycles.ts` - passed, 0 runtime value cycles.
- `OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 OPENCLAW_LIVE_OLLAMA=1 OPENCLAW_LIVE_OLLAMA_MODEL=qwen2.5:3b OPENCLAW_LIVE_OLLAMA_WEB_SEARCH=0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.live.config.ts extensions/ollama/ollama.live.test.ts -t "runs native chat"` - passed, 1 live test / 3 skipped by filter.
- Manual local source CLI proof with a custom provider whose provider-level API/baseUrl points at an inert router and whose selected model overrides to native local Ollama - passed.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: custom/router-backed model selections now carry the final resolved transport/auth/stream facts into auth, stream setup, and runtime planning. A provider default no longer stands in for a per-model runtime choice.

Real environment tested: local source checkout with Node 22, repo dependencies installed, and local Ollama serving `qwen2.5:3b` at `http://127.0.0.1:11434`. No external provider secrets were printed or committed.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: created a temporary `openclaw.json` where `models.providers.custom-router` had provider-level `api: "openai-completions"` and `baseUrl: "https://router.invalid/v1"`, while selected model `custom-router/qwen2.5:3b` had model-level `api: "ollama"` and `baseUrl: "http://127.0.0.1:11434"`; then ran:

```bash
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$tmp/state" \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$tmp/openclaw.json" \
OPENCLAW_NO_RESPAWN=1 \
OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 \
OPENCLAW_LIVE_OLLAMA=1 \
OLLAMA_API_KEY= \
node scripts/run-node.mjs infer model run \
  --local \
  --model custom-router/qwen2.5:3b \
  --prompt 'Reply with exactly one word: pong' \
  --json
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "capability": "model.run",
  "transport": "local",
  "provider": "custom-router",
  "model": "qwen2.5:3b",
  "attempts": [],
  "outputs": [
    {
      "text": "pong",
      "mediaUrl": null
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: OpenClaw kept the visible selected provider/model as `custom-router/qwen2.5:3b`, but used the selected model's native Ollama API/base URL for the actual local run. The inert provider-level router URL was not used, and the command returned `ok: true` with the local model response.

What was not tested: full remote `pnpm check:changed` proof is blocked in this environment because Blacksmith Testbox is unavailable (`blacksmith` CLI missing) and direct AWS Crabbox has no usable AWS credentials/IMDS identity. Live Anthropic, Google, and OpenAI auth-profile smokes were not run in this local draft pass.

## Risks

- This is an internal descriptor plumbing change, so the main risk is stale callers continuing to infer runtime facts from only provider/model strings.
- Plugin auth hook contexts receive additive optional fields only.

target.title: Doctor: continue structured repair conversion
target.body:
## Goal

Move plugin registry and configured plugin install repairs into the structured Doctor repair contract started by #83753.

This wave is about plugin repair policy that used to live in command-specific flows: detect the broken plugin registry or configured install state, pass those exact findings into repair, and report the repair as structured `changes`, `warnings`, `effects`, and diffs where file edits are available.

Notes are threaded through like the rest of OpenClaw: presentation only through `ctx.doctor.note(...)`; no `detect().notes`, no `repair().notes`, no runner note arrays.

## Legacy To Modern Shape

Legacy plugin doctor repairs mixed registry detection, configured install checks, prompting, and output text in the same flow. This PR keeps the existing behavior and contribution order, but makes the result isomorphic:

- lint and repair start from the same plugin findings
- repair only acts on the findings it receives
- dry-run can describe the plugin install/config changes without mutating state
- repair output records effects and warnings instead of relying on presentation text
- note text remains suppressible presentation, not machine signal

## Converted Here

- plugin registry doctor checks
- configured plugin install doctor repairs
- legacy plugin manifest contract repairs
- focused plugin doctor tests around detection, repair, dry-run, diffs, effects, and detect-after validation

## Review Surface

This is the plugin-policy PR to challenge before the rest of the stack assumes the shape:

- whether plugin install findings are grouped at the right level: registry entry, configured plugin, manifest, or install root
- whether repair effects should identify plugin ids, package refs, manifest files, or filesystem targets differently
- whether registry/config repair should expose more concrete diffs for config writes
- whether any plugin-specific policy is leaking into generic Doctor runner code

## Stack Position

Parent: #83753 at `9c35ac54686c0e3c4448208f3f448235e6ae2af6`. This PR is the plugin registry/configured install wave in the Doctor repair stack.

Follow-on PRs continue the same contract through sandbox, gateway services, session state, config audit/cron, and finally the top-level dry-run preview surface.

## Contract Examples In This PR

| Repair area | Detects | Repairs | Dry-run / diff / effect proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Plugin registry repairs | registry entries or installed plugin records that no longer match the expected plugin contract | rewrites or removes the same plugin registry/config state the legacy doctor path handled | `doctor --fix --dry-run --diff` reports the planned plugin registry/config action; file-backed rewrites can expose textual diffs, while install-root or registry side effects are reported as effects instead of fake patches |
| Configured plugin install repairs | configured plugin installs that are missing, stale, or no longer valid for the current manifest/package shape | runs the existing configured-install repair policy for only the selected findings | dry-run reports the package/plugin target and planned install/config action before mutation; warnings stay machine-readable `warnings`, not note text |
| Legacy plugin manifest contract repairs | legacy manifest/package metadata that needs the modern plugin contract shape | applies the same manifest cleanup/rewrite that the legacy doctor flow performed | `--diff` is meaningful for file-backed manifest edits; non-file install checks report effects and empty textual diffs |

These examples are the plugin-policy version of the base contract: same findings for lint and repair, selected findings passed into repair, concrete dry-run output before mutation, diffs only for real file edits, and effects for plugin install or registry side effects.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node node_modules\vitest\vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-manifests.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts`
- The repo Vitest wrapper initially hit `spawn EPERM` in this isolated worktree after dependency install, so the same focused files were run through Vitest directly.
- Fresh PR CI should provide broad proof across the full stack.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Plugin registry, configured plugin install, and legacy plugin manifest repairs now use finding-scoped structured repair output while preserving existing plugin repair behavior.

Real environment tested: Windows isolated worktree at commit `ab23b05cee888c929f5332f20f241b760c6334f1`, restacked on #83753.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node node_modules\vitest\vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-manifests.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: 5 focused Doctor/plugin test files passed, 69 tests total, covering plugin registry detection/repair, legacy manifest rewrites, configured plugin install dry-run/diff/effects, positional contribution ordering, and detect-after validation.

Observed result after fix: Plugin repair behavior remains available through Doctor, while dry-run/repair callers can consume structured repair output instead of presentation text. Notes stay presentation-only.

What was not tested: Full plugin install live scenarios and platform-wide Doctor CI were not rerun locally for this body refresh.

target.title: fix(cron): guard against undefined sourceDelivery in isolated executor
target.body:
## Summary

Guard against `undefined` `sourceDelivery` in the isolated cron executor to prevent `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')`.

## Problem

Commit `4c613fbfe0` ("refactor(cron): centralize source delivery plan", 2026-05-18) replaced three separate params (`messageChannel`, `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, `toolPolicy`) with a unified `sourceDelivery: SourceDeliveryPlan` across the lazy-import boundary between `run.ts` and `run-executor.ts`.

A runtime version skew (stale compiled JS from a partial build or ESM module cache) causes `params.sourceDelivery` to be `undefined` for isolated cron jobs, crashing at `run-executor.ts:147`.

## Fix

1. Made `sourceDelivery` optional in both `createCronPromptExecutor` and `executeCronRun`
2. When `sourceDelivery` is undefined, reconstruct a `SourceDeliveryPlan` that **preserves legacy caller fields**:
   - Reads `toolPolicy` from stale caller params (`disableMessageTool`, `forceMessageTool`, `requireExplicitMessageTarget`)
   - Reads `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` from stale caller params
   - Reads `messageChannel` from stale caller params
   - Falls back to `resolvedDelivery` for channel/to/accountId/threadId
3. Added `logWarn` diagnostic to flag the fallback

This ensures webhook callers that disabled the message tool still have it disabled, announce callers that set `message_tool_only` keep that mode, and no security boundary is widened.

## Tests

5 test cases in `run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts`:
1. `sourceDelivery: undefined` (no legacy fields) → reconstructs with safe defaults ✅
2. `sourceDelivery: undefined` → falls back to `resolvedDelivery` channel/to/accountId/threadId ✅
3. Valid `sourceDelivery` → works normally (regression) ✅
4. **Stale announce caller**: `sourceDelivery: undefined` + `toolPolicy: { disableMessageTool: false, forceMessageTool: true }` + `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"` → preserves those values ✅
5. **Stale webhook caller**: `sourceDelivery: undefined` + `toolPolicy: { disableMessageTool: true, forceMessageTool: false }` → message tool stays disabled ✅

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: All `sessionTarget: "isolated"` cron jobs crash with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')`
- **Real environment tested**: Docker containers built from OpenClaw source, Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js v24.16.0

### Failing proof (upstream/main, before fix)

Built Docker image from upstream/main and ran `executeCronRun` from compiled `dist/` with `sourceDelivery: undefined`:

```
$ docker run --rm openclaw-main-proof "node scripts/cron-proof.mjs"

=== Issue #85051 Real Behavior Proof ===
Environment: linux arm64 Node v24.16.0

Loading: run-executor.runtime-CmqBcLXN.js
Test: executeCronRun with sourceDelivery=undefined

❌ CRASH (sourceDelivery bug): TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')
   This is the exact bug reported in issue #85051
TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')
    at createCronPromptExecutor (file:///app/dist/run-executor.runtime-CmqBcLXN.js:81:56)
    at executeCronRun (file:///app/dist/run-executor.runtime-CmqBcLXN.js:237:19)
```

### Passing proof (fix branch, after fix)

```
$ docker run --rm openclaw-cron-fix-v4 "node scripts/cron-proof.mjs"

=== Issue #85051 Real Behavior Proof ===
Environment: linux arm64 Node v24.16.0

Loading: run-executor.runtime-Cl47DXeI.js
Test: executeCronRun with sourceDelivery=undefined

[cron:proof-job] sourceDelivery is undefined; using fallback — possible build artifact mismatch
✅ PASSED sourceDelivery guard
   (subsequent error is expected without LLM config: All models failed...)
```

### Docker vitest proof (all 5 tests including legacy caller shapes)

```
$ docker run --rm openclaw-cron-fix-v4 "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts"

✓ cron ../../src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts (5 tests) 47ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  5 passed (5)
  Duration  843ms
```

Fixes #85051

target.title: fix(prompt-cache): preserve prefix across hook/runtime context
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` (4 call sites) and the `systemPromptOverrideText` runtime-extra-context branch wrote hook/runtime context ABOVE the system-prompt cache-boundary marker. The downstream `anthropic-payload-policy.ts` splits on the marker to place `cache_control`, so the per-turn-varying hook content ended up inside the stable-prefix region and invalidated the prompt cache on every turn. OpenAI auto prefix cache hit the same prefix-byte-stability requirement and missed identically. Issue #85203 measured ~50-65% cache hit vs achievable 88-94%.
- Solution: route hook prepend, hook append, and runtime-extra-context additions through the cache-boundary helper so they land in the dynamic suffix region. Add a missing `appendSystemPromptAdditionAfterCacheBoundary` (existing helper covered only prepend) and `ensureSystemPromptCacheBoundary` (idempotent boundary synthesis for top-level prompt assembly boundaries). Both helpers strip embedded marker substrings from additions to prevent a smuggled sentinel reaching providers via the dynamic suffix.
- What changed: 7 files, +342 / -21 lines across 2 commits. Commit 1 (`a100ed525d`) lands the helpers + refactor + base regression tests; commit 2 (`2f5042653d`) adds stripped-bytes lock-in assertions for the two disclosed visible-byte changes (markerless append-only single-newline join; override+extraSystemPrompt single-newline gap between override text and subagent context). New helpers in `src/agents/system-prompt-cache-boundary.ts`; `composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` refactored to chain ensure → prepend → append; `appendRuntimeExtraSystemPrompt` (override branch) routed through the same helpers.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Sites 1, 2, 4, 5 from #85203 (they operate on user-turn text, not system prompt) are NOT touched. Site 7 (`appendFileBlocks`) operates on inbound message body, also not touched. Site 6's generated-system-prompt path (the fragment lands in the outer dynamic region below the marker emitted at `system-prompt.ts:1239`) is correct as-is and is NOT touched. Google managed cache paths are out of scope per the issue. The legacy no-marker fallback inside `prependSystemPromptAdditionAfterCacheBoundary` is left unchanged for any pre-existing caller that may rely on it.

## Motivation

The cache-boundary marker was introduced precisely to keep the system-prompt stable prefix byte-identical across turns so providers can hit prompt cache from turn 2 onward. Two paths bypassed it. The cost is measurable: every turn re-creates the cache instead of reading it, multiplying the input-token bill by N for an N-turn conversation and burning provider cache-creation latency on every step.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #85203
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

The issue is not auto-closed because the issue body enumerates 6 dist:line sites; this PR ships the 2 actually-broken system-prompt paths (`composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` and the override branch) plus the missing `appendSystemPromptAdditionAfterCacheBoundary` helper. Sites 1/2/4/5/7 operate on different surfaces (user-turn text or inbound message body) and are out of scope. Maintainers may close #85203 after merge if the analysis above is accepted, or keep it open to track any of the unaddressed surfaces if they choose.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:

The bug class: when `composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` was used with a varying hook prepend/append (typical case: session/runtime context that changes per turn), the hook content landed BEFORE the cache-boundary marker in the assembled system prompt. The provider-side `anthropic-payload-policy.ts` splits the system prompt on the marker to decide where to place the `cache_control` breakpoint; with hook content in the stable-prefix region every turn produced a different prefix and the cache missed on turn 2+. OpenAI's auto prefix cache has the same prefix-byte-stability requirement and was broken identically. The `systemPromptOverrideText` runtime-extra-context branch bypassed the helper entirely and emitted a fully markerless prompt, so override + subagent context broke prompt cache on every subagent spawn.

- Real environment tested:

Standalone Node ESM proof script (`/tmp/proof-cache.mjs`) imports `composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` and the boundary helpers from BOTH the build of this branch and the build of `upstream/main 6bd430ee35`, sends 3-turn sequences with varying hook content to live provider APIs, and captures cache token counters from real responses. The script reuses the production `applyAnthropicCacheControlToSystem` block-split logic (`splitSystemPromptCacheBoundary` → two-block system with `cache_control` on the stable-prefix block only) so the Anthropic payload shape is identical to what OpenClaw emits at runtime.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
pnpm -F openclaw build         # V3 worktree (this branch)
pnpm -F openclaw build         # baseline worktree at upstream/main 6bd430ee35
node /tmp/proof-cache.mjs      # imports both dists, hits both providers, prints results
```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture, copied live output):

Stable-prefix byte equality across the 3 turns (extracted via `splitSystemPromptCacheBoundary` on the composed system prompt):

```
baseline stable_prefix_byte_identical_across_turns = false
baseline stable_prefix_lengths = [22843, 22840, 22841]

V3       stable_prefix_byte_identical_across_turns = true
V3       stable_prefix_lengths = [22736, 22736, 22736]
```

Anthropic `/v1/messages` response usage (cache-eligible Anthropic model, 5-minute ephemeral cache):

```
                  baseline                              V3
turn 1   cache_creation=5045  cache_read=0    cache_creation=5006  cache_read=0
turn 2   cache_creation=5045  cache_read=0    cache_creation=0     cache_read=5006
turn 3   cache_creation=5045  cache_read=0    cache_creation=0     cache_read=5006
```

OpenAI `/v1/chat/completions` response usage (cache-eligible OpenAI model, auto prefix cache):

```
                  baseline                              V3
turn 1   prompt=4840  cached=0               prompt=4840  cached=0
turn 2   prompt=4844  cached=0               prompt=4844  cached=4736
turn 3   prompt=4844  cached=0               prompt=4844  cached=4736
```

- Observed result after fix:

Baseline re-creates the cache on every turn (`cache_creation` on every turn, `cache_read=0` on every turn) on Anthropic and reports `cached_tokens=0` on all 3 turns on OpenAI. V3 writes the cache on turn 1 and reads the full stable prefix from cache on turns 2 and 3 (Anthropic `cache_read=5006`, ~99% of input; OpenAI `cached_tokens=4736`, ~98% of prompt). The stable prefix is byte-identical across all 3 turns and only the dynamic suffix (hook content + dynamic base context) varies.

- What was not tested:
    - Subagent override branch (`attempt-system-prompt.ts`) cache behavior was not exercised against live providers — it is covered by unit tests (`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt-system-prompt.test.ts`) that assert the new byte sequence and marker-aware composition.
    - Google managed cache paths (out of scope per the issue body — those providers use server-side conversation cache, not prompt-prefix cache).
    - Long-TTL (`extended-cache-ttl-2025-04-11`) was not exercised; the proof used the default 5-minute ephemeral TTL because the 3 turns fit comfortably in that window.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Two system-prompt mutation paths bypassed the cache-boundary helper. `composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` (4 call sites in the prepare + attempt runners) concatenated hook prepend/append text around the base system prompt with no marker-aware logic, so when the base contained the marker the hook content landed in the stable-prefix region. The `systemPromptOverrideText` branch bypassed the generated system-prompt builder entirely and emitted no marker at all, so override + subagent context was a fully markerless prompt and could never be split for cache_control placement.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no regression test asserted byte-stable stable prefix across turns when hook context varied. The existing `system-prompt-cache-boundary.test.ts` covered the helper in isolation but not the helper's callers.
- Contributing context: `appendSystemPromptAdditionAfterCacheBoundary` did not exist; only the prepend variant was defined. The override-branch refactor needed both directions to remain readable.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
    - [x] Unit test
    - [x] Seam / integration test
    - [ ] End-to-end test
    - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
    - `src/agents/system-prompt-cache-boundary.test.ts` (helper-level + cache-prefix-stability regression)
    - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts` (interaction: hook prepend + hook append in the same turn through the full compose chain)
    - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt-system-prompt.test.ts` (override-branch byte sequence + marker-precedes-subagent-context + smuggled-marker-strip)
    - `src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts` (compose invoked with expected argument shape from the prepare call site)
- Scenario the test should lock in: with a marker-bearing base system prompt and a hook prepend that varies per turn, the bytes BEFORE the marker (the stable prefix) must remain byte-identical across turns; smuggled marker substrings inside additions must be stripped so `splitSystemPromptCacheBoundary` (which uses `indexOf` and matches the first marker only) cannot be misled; AND the two intentional post-strip visible-byte changes (markerless append-only single-newline; override+extra single-newline gap) are asserted on the stripped output so a future helper change cannot silently alter the disclosed contract.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: a single-process unit test that compares stable-prefix bytes across two compose calls catches the regression deterministically with no provider/network dependency.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): the helper-level tests in `src/agents/system-prompt-cache-boundary.test.ts` existed but did not assert the prepend+append composition path or the override-branch path. Both gaps are filled.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A — new tests added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Three intentional, disclosed byte-level changes:

1. Hook prepend/append context now appears AFTER the (synthesized) cache-boundary marker. Previous byte order was `prepend + base + append`; new byte order is `base-stable-region + MARKER + prepend + base-dynamic-region + append`. This is the intended fix — the entire point of routing through the helper is to keep the stable prefix byte-identical across turns. Visible to the model only after `stripSystemPromptCacheBoundary` collapses the marker into `\n`.

2. Append-only paths use a single-newline separator between the stable prefix and the appended addition AFTER the downstream provider strip. The new helper emits `stablePrefix + MARKER + addition` when the input has no dynamic suffix; once `stripSystemPromptCacheBoundary` runs in the provider adapter, the marker collapses to `\n`, so providers see a single newline between the two regions. Previous naive append patterns in this codebase used a literal `\n\n` separator with no marker.

3. Override + extraSystemPrompt visible bytes change from `OVERRIDE\n\n## Subagent Context\n<extra>\n` to `OVERRIDE\n## Subagent Context\n<extra>` because the override branch now routes through `ensureSystemPromptCacheBoundary` + `appendSystemPromptAdditionAfterCacheBoundary`. The override path previously assembled the suffix with literal `\n\n` separators and a trailing newline; the helper-based assembly drops the trailing newline and emits the join via the marker (which the provider then strips to `\n`). Existing tests at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt-system-prompt.test.ts` are updated to reflect the new byte sequence.

## Diagram

```text
Before (baseline):
  [HOOK_PREPEND]──┐
                  ├─► concat ─► [HOOK_PREPEND │ STABLE_BASE ╎MARKER╎ DYNAMIC_BASE │ HOOK_APPEND]
  [BASE w/ MARK]──┤                            ─────────────────┬───────────────  
  [HOOK_APPEND ]──┘                            stablePrefix on split (varies per turn)

After (V3):
  ensure(BASE)         →  [STABLE_BASE ╎MARKER╎ DYNAMIC_BASE]
  prepend(HOOK_PREPEND)→  [STABLE_BASE ╎MARKER╎ HOOK_PREPEND │ DYNAMIC_BASE]
  append (HOOK_APPEND) →  [STABLE_BASE ╎MARKER╎ HOOK_PREPEND │ DYNAMIC_BASE │ HOOK_APPEND]
                          ───────┬──────                     
                          stablePrefix on split (byte-stable across turns)
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A.

Marker-substring sanitization in both helpers (full marker + trimmed comment form) is a defense-in-depth hardening: a hook or extra-context value that happens to contain the sentinel can no longer reach the provider verbatim or fool the downstream `splitSystemPromptCacheBoundary` (which uses `indexOf` and matches only the first marker). No new attack surface is introduced.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.17.0-14-generic
- Runtime/container: Node 22.22.1, pnpm
- Model/provider: provider-agnostic — applies to all providers consuming the system-prompt cache-boundary marker (Anthropic-family + OpenAI-family). Proof script used live cache-eligible models from both families.
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A (system-prompt assembly path).
- Relevant config (redacted): default cache retention (`short` / 5-min ephemeral on Anthropic; auto on OpenAI). `OPENAI_API_KEY` from env; Anthropic via Max OAuth token.

### Steps

1. Build both worktrees (V3 branch + baseline at `upstream/main 6bd430ee35`).
2. Construct a deterministic ~22.7 KB stable base system prompt with the marker emitted at the same position the production builder emits it.
3. For each (version × turn), call `composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` from that version's dist with a hook prepend/append that varies per turn (simulating session/runtime hook context).
4. For Anthropic: replicate `applyAnthropicCacheControlToSystem` (split on marker → two-block system, `cache_control: ephemeral` on stable-prefix block only) and POST to `/v1/messages`.
5. For OpenAI: strip the marker and POST the resulting plain text as the system message to `/v1/chat/completions`.
6. Capture `usage.cache_creation_input_tokens` / `usage.cache_read_input_tokens` (Anthropic) and `usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens` (OpenAI) per turn.

### Expected

- Baseline: stable prefix bytes vary per turn (hook content in stable-prefix region) → cache_read = 0 on every turn → cache_creation paid on every turn.
- V3: stable prefix bytes byte-identical across turns → cache_read ≈ full stable-prefix size from turn 2 onward.

### Actual

Matches Expected — see the Real behavior proof section above for full numbers (baseline turn 2/3 cache_read = 0 / cached = 0; V3 turn 2/3 cache_read = 5006 on Anthropic, cached = 4736 on OpenAI).

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [x] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Unit + interaction tests cover the V3-affected code paths: 370/370 passing across `system-prompt-cache-boundary.test.ts`, `attempt.test.ts`, `attempt-system-prompt.test.ts`, `cli-runner/prepare.test.ts`. `pnpm check:changed` (typecheck + lint + import cycles) clean.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
    - Built both V3 and baseline worktrees from clean state, ran the proof script against live provider APIs, observed the cache_read / cached_tokens numbers above.
    - Confirmed stable-prefix byte equality across the 3 V3 turns via `splitSystemPromptCacheBoundary` on the composed system prompt.
    - Ran the 4 V3-affected test files (`system-prompt-cache-boundary.test.ts`, `attempt.test.ts`, `attempt-system-prompt.test.ts`, `cli-runner/prepare.test.ts`); all green after the lock-in assertions were added.
    - Verified `pnpm check:changed` runs clean on the V3 worktree.
- Edge cases checked:
    - Marker-bearing extraSystemPrompt: the smuggled sentinel is stripped by both prepend and append helpers (covered by the new `attempt-system-prompt.test.ts` strip test).
    - Markerless input with empty dynamic suffix: helper emits `stablePrefix + MARKER + addition` (single-newline after provider strip); covered by unit test.
    - Override-without-extraSystemPrompt: remains markerless (no helper invocation when there is no addition).
    - Existing `prependSystemPromptAdditionAfterCacheBoundary` no-marker fallback path: untouched; all V3 callers route through `ensureSystemPromptCacheBoundary` first so they never trigger that legacy fallback.
- What you did **not** verify:
    - Live-provider cache behavior of the override branch (covered by unit-test assertions on byte sequence + marker precedence, not by real provider calls).
    - Long-TTL (1h `extended-cache-ttl-2025-04-11`) — the 3-turn proof window fits comfortably inside the default 5-min ephemeral cache.
    - Google managed cache (out of scope).
    - Wider test suite was not re-run as part of this PR's verification; `pnpm check:changed` + the 4 V3-affected test files were the focused gate. Unrelated pre-existing baseline failures exist on `upstream/main 6bd430ee35` (8 in `compact.hooks.test.ts` + `model.forward-compat.errors-and-overrides.test.ts`); not introduced by this PR.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

(PR not yet opened — this section will be applicable after first bot review pass.)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` (all exported helper signatures preserved; `composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` signature unchanged; the existing prepend helper's behavior unchanged for callers that already passed marker-bearing bases).
- Config/env changes? `No`.
- Migration needed? `No`.

The 3 intentional byte-level visible changes (see User-visible / Behavior Changes) are downstream byte-shape changes inside the system prompt the model receives. They do not change any exported API, config, or env contract.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a third-party plugin or external caller could be invoking `prependSystemPromptAdditionAfterCacheBoundary` directly on a fragment that does not contain the marker, relying on the legacy "prepend-as-fallback" behavior (the helper currently prepends the addition rather than appending it when no marker is found).
    - Mitigation: legacy no-marker fallback in the prepend helper is left untouched. All in-tree callers now route through `ensureSystemPromptCacheBoundary` first, so they never trigger that fallback; external callers continue to get the existing behavior.
- Risk: override-branch byte change (`OVERRIDE\n\n## Subagent Context...\n` → `OVERRIDE\n## Subagent Context...`) could matter for any downstream consumer that pattern-matches the override+subagent prompt as a literal string.
    - Mitigation: existing fixture and the updated `attempt-system-prompt.test.ts` assertions lock in the new shape; the change is disclosed under User-visible / Behavior Changes. If a consumer needs the old shape, the override branch is one helper call away from being adjusted.

---

AI-assisted; contributor manually reviewed and is responsible for the change.

target.title: Fix heartbeat message-tool delivery policy
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #85310.
- Aligns scheduled heartbeat response-tool selection with group/channel `messages.groupChat.visibleReplies` policy.
- Includes model-specific heartbeat runtime resolution so `agents.defaults.models[model].agentRuntime.id = "codex"` activates message-tool-only heartbeat delivery.

## Root Cause
Scheduled heartbeat runs decided whether to force the heartbeat response tool before carrying forward the resolved delivery chat type or model-specific runtime policy. That meant a group/channel setup using `messages.groupChat.visibleReplies = "message_tool"`, or a model-specific Codex runtime override, could still run with the legacy `HEARTBEAT_OK` prompt and no `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` override. Downstream dispatch already suppresses text-only failed tool output when `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` is `message_tool_only`; the heartbeat path was not consistently entering that runtime-enforced mode.

## Why This Is Safe
- The change only affects heartbeat prompt/delivery mode selection for already-configured message-tool-only or Codex-runtime heartbeat cases.
- Existing automatic heartbeat behavior remains unchanged when visible replies are `automatic` and no Codex runtime policy applies.
- Model-specific non-Codex runtime config continues to override broader default Codex heartbeat mode, with regression coverage.
- The fix reuses existing runtime policy resolution and existing dispatch suppression rather than adding channel-specific string filtering.

## Security And Runtime Controls
- Existing send policy checks are unchanged.
- Existing message-tool availability/profile policy is unchanged.
- Existing dispatch-side `message_tool_only` suppression for text-only failed tool output is unchanged.
- No new channel credentials, allowlist behavior, provider auth, or tool permissions are introduced.

## Real Behavior Proof
Behavior addressed: heartbeat scheduled turns now honor group/channel `message_tool` reply policy and model-specific Codex runtime config, so failed internal tool/file-read messages stay private.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on this PR branch, with a temporary in-memory config that enabled only `channels.discord`. Telegram, Slack, Matrix, Signal, iMessage, MS Teams, and WhatsApp were disabled by omission. Discord token, guild ID, channel ID, and message IDs are redacted below.

After fix, OpenClaw sent a real heartbeat notification into the configured Discord channel and the proof script read the channel back through the Discord API:

Discord message URL for manual verification: https://discord.com/channels/1481298384714338398/1481298387058950239/1507377049021448192

A local browser screenshot was also captured from that URL (`discord-issue-85310-proof.png`) showing the proof message in `#general` on the `openclaw test server` Discord. The screenshot is not committed to the repo.


```json
{
  "proof": "issue-85310-real-discord-after",
  "sanitizedConfig": {
    "enabledChannels": ["discord"],
    "disabledByOmission": [
      "telegram",
      "slack",
      "matrix",
      "signal",
      "imessage",
      "msteams",
      "whatsapp"
    ],
    "guildId": "1481...8398",
    "channelId": "1481...0239",
    "token": "REDACTED",
    "messages": {
      "groupChat": {
        "visibleReplies": "message_tool"
      }
    },
    "model": {
      "primary": "openai/gpt-5.5"
    },
    "modelRuntime": {
      "id": "codex"
    },
    "discordStreamingMode": "off"
  },
  "result": {
    "status": "ran",
    "reason": null
  },
  "captured": {
    "bodyHasHeartbeatRespond": true,
    "bodyHasLegacyOk": false,
    "sourceReplyDeliveryMode": "message_tool_only",
    "enableHeartbeatTool": true,
    "forceHeartbeatTool": true
  },
  "discordReadback": {
    "sentMessageFound": true,
    "sentMessageId": "1507...8192",
    "sentMessageContent": "OpenClaw Discord proof oc85310-1779457093435-665a9c: heartbeat message-tool delivery is active.",
    "newMessageCount": 1,
    "newMessages": [
      {
        "id": "1507...8192",
        "authorBot": true,
        "content": "OpenClaw Discord proof oc85310-1779457093435-665a9c: heartbeat message-tool delivery is active."
      }
    ],
    "leakedInternalMarkerCount": 0,
    "leakedInternalMarkers": []
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: the real Discord channel received only the expected OpenClaw heartbeat proof message. The heartbeat runner entered `message_tool_only` runtime mode, used the heartbeat response tool prompt instead of the legacy `HEARTBEAT_OK` prompt, and the Discord readback found no `print lines ... failed`, `HEARTBEAT_OK`, or `heartbeat_respond` internal markers in the new visible channel output.

## Tests
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `pnpm build`

## Out Of Scope
- No Discord-specific sanitizer or output string filtering.
- No changes to channel allowlists, credentials, send policy, or tool permission policy.
- No broad rewrite of the heartbeat runner or dispatch pipeline.

## Transparency
AI-assisted implementation; reviewed against the final diff and validated with the commands above.

target.title: Preserve Codex and Telegram context evidence
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram prompt context cached inbound messages but not OpenClaw's own outbound Telegram replies, so a later context window could include Keshav's 01:37 request while omitting the adjacent 01:39 assistant answer.
- Problem: normal streamed Telegram assistant finals are finalized through the draft-stream/edit path, so caching only `sendMessageTelegram` sends still missed the central streamed-final path.
- Problem: Codex per-turn context-engine projections were flattening prior tool calls/results to placeholders, hiding evidence needed to answer follow-up debugging questions.
- Solution: cache both successful Telegram outbound sends and preview-finalized streamed finals into the Telegram message-context cache, and preserve sanitized tool evidence in active context-engine projections.
- Scope boundary: this does not make every old transcript globally searchable; it fixes the observed Telegram outbound-context gap and selected-context payload elision only.

## Motivation

Keshav pointed out that a later Codex turn did not show the earlier timeout-halving work or the 01:39 final answer evidence. Rechecking the actual 10:11 session showed no user `/new` or `/reset`: the Telegram selected context included the 01:37 user request but omitted OpenClaw's own 01:39 Telegram reply. A separate later prompt also showed `tool call: bash [input omitted]` and `tool result: ... [content omitted]`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #85219
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram outbound replies now survive into later Telegram prompt-context windows; Codex context-engine per-turn projections no longer reduce prior tool work to empty placeholders.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on this host, branch `fix/codex-context-tool-payloads`, head commit `8e3ea2be9e3462d3d60da14cf2290ccc796e2dc7`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran targeted Vitest coverage from the repo root. Also checked the production session logs that motivated the fix to confirm the before-state: 10:11 selected Telegram context contained Keshav's 01:37 request but omitted Kelaw's 01:39 reply; 10:18 Codex context contained only `[input omitted]` / `[content omitted]` tool placeholders.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied local output from the patched checkout:

```text
Test Files 4 passed (4)
Tests 218 passed (218)
```

- Observed result after fix: The Telegram send regression records a bot reply in a forum topic, then the next selected Telegram conversation context contains that bot reply body. The streamed-final regression records a preview-finalized assistant reply from the draft-stream path, then a later Telegram context window selects that assistant reply body. The projected Codex prompt includes useful result text like `already halved timeoutSeconds=7200`, includes tool input shape metadata, does not include `[content omitted]`, and does not expose raw shell command or secret-like token.
- What was not tested: A full live Telegram-to-Codex production turn was not exercised locally. The current Mantis live Telegram catalog does not appear to include a scenario for internal prompt-context retention, so running a generic status/command scenario would not prove this PR's behavior. A stronger live proof would require a dedicated QA scenario or a candidate deployment probe.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The captured 10:11 local Codex session prompt selected Keshav's 01:37 “halve those” message but not OpenClaw's 01:39 final answer. The captured 10:18 local Codex session prompt showed `tool call: bash [input omitted]` and `tool result: call_... [content omitted]`.

- Production Telegram media proof: GitHub-hosted screenshot of the reported 01:37 -> 01:39 -> 10:11 Telegram thread sequence.

  ![Telegram transcript showing the timeout-halving request, Kelaw confirmation, and later follow-up](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/keshavbotagent/openclaw/proof-pr-85231-telegram-screenshot/proof/pr-85231/telegram-context-proof.jpg)

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Telegram only recorded inbound messages in `message-cache.ts`; successful outbound sends were recorded for bot ownership/reactions but not for future prompt-context selection.
- Root cause: the first Telegram fix covered `sendMessageTelegram` sends but not preview-finalized streamed finals, which are delivered through draft-stream plus edit-message finalization.
- Root cause: `run-attempt.ts` passed `toolPayloadMode: contextEngineProjection ? "preserve" : "elide"`, so active per-turn context-engine projections used elision even when the projection engine had safe redaction support.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No regression covered Telegram outbound replies being present in later selected conversation context, streamed finals being written to the same cache, or active per-turn context-engine projections preserving redacted tool-result evidence.

## Prompt Boundary Review

ClawSweeper flagged the prompt-boundary expansion in `run-attempt.ts`. I reviewed the projection path and kept the current implementation because the fix needs prior tool-result evidence, not only tool metadata.

Boundary details:

- Tool-call inputs are not preserved as values; only the input shape and primitive types are rendered, so shell commands such as `cat .env` are omitted.
- Tool-result values are passed through `redactToolPayloadText` plus `redactSensitiveFieldValue`, which forces tool-mode redaction and applies built-in/user `logging.redactPatterns`.
- Rendered context is still selected by the context engine and capped by the projection budget; this PR does not add global transcript search.
- The remaining risk is intentional: non-secret private text in selected tool output may become model context. Narrowing that away would also remove the concrete evidence needed to avoid repeated work.

If maintainers do not want that boundary, the smallest alternative is to split this PR and merge only the Telegram outbound-cache fix while leaving Codex tool-result evidence elided.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`, `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: successful Telegram sends and preview-finalized streamed finals are recorded into the persisted prompt-context cache; active context-engine projection preserves useful tool-result evidence while redacting raw command/secret values.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it covers both Telegram delivery paths that can produce visible assistant replies and the Codex prompt sent to `turn/start`.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `context-engine-projection.test.ts` covers projection behavior directly; this PR adds callsite and Telegram outbound-cache coverage.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Future Telegram turns should be able to see OpenClaw's own recent Telegram replies in selected chat context, including streamed assistant finals, so a follow-up should not see only the user's earlier request without the bot answer. Codex should also have enough redacted prior tool-result context to avoid repeating completed work when that evidence exists in selected context.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `pnpm check:test-types`

target.title: fix(cli): resolve gateway host from bind mode and surface auth hint on silent disconnect
target.body:
## Problem

Two related bugs that make the gateway unusable with CLI tools when non-default bind or auth settings are configured:

### Bug 1: CLI fails silently when `gateway.auth.mode = "token"` is set

When a user configures token auth in `openclaw.json`, all CLI tools (`openclaw cron list`, `openclaw sessions list`, etc.) fail with:
```
gateway closed (1000 normal closure): no close reason
```
There is no indication that authentication is required. The user has no idea what went wrong.

### Bug 2: CLI hardcodes `127.0.0.1` regardless of `gateway.bind` mode

When `gateway.bind = "tailnet"`, the gateway listens on the Tailscale IP (e.g. `100.88.110.43`) — not loopback. But CLI tools always try `ws://127.0.0.1:18789` and fail to connect entirely.

Same issue applies to `gateway.bind = "custom"` with a `customBindHost`.

## Fix

**`src/gateway/call.ts`**

1. Added `resolveLocalHostForBind()` — derives the correct connection host from `gateway.bind` config:
   - `tailnet` → `pickPrimaryTailnetIPv4()` (already used elsewhere in the codebase)
   - `custom` → `gateway.customBindHost`
   - `loopback` / `lan` / anything else → `127.0.0.1` (existing behaviour preserved)

2. Added auth hint to `formatGatewayCloseError()` — when the gateway closes with code 1000 or 1008 and no reason, and the config has auth enabled but no token was resolved, appends:
   ```
   Authentication required — pass --token or configure gateway.auth.token in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
   ```

## Reproducer

```json
// ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
{
  "gateway": {
    "bind": "tailnet",
    "auth": { "mode": "token", "token": "your-token" }
  }
}
```

Then run `openclaw cron list` — previously failed silently, now works correctly (or gives a clear auth error if the token is missing).

## Notes

- No new dependencies
- `pickPrimaryTailnetIPv4` is already imported elsewhere in the codebase
- Behaviour is fully backwards-compatible — loopback bind still targets `127.0.0.1`

target.title: Policy: add secret and auth conformance checks
target.body:
## Summary

Adds Policy 1.0 secret provenance and auth-profile conformance checks with redacted config evidence.

Logical base: merged #80783. This PR intentionally stays config-level only: it reads existing OpenClaw config and reports policy conformance findings through `doctor --lint`; it does not add runtime enforcement and it does not take on secrets management.

- Records managed SecretRef/provider provenance without storing secret values.
- Reports unmanaged SecretRefs, denied secret provider sources, insecure secret provider posture, missing auth profile metadata, and unapproved auth profile modes.
- Documents the offered policy syntax and evidence shape.

## Policy syntax

```jsonc
{
  "secrets": {
    "requireManagedProviders": true,
    "denySources": ["exec"],
    "allowInsecureProviders": false
  },
  "auth": {
    "profiles": {
      "requireMetadata": ["provider", "mode"],
      "allowModes": ["api_key", "token"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Safety

This is policy conformance, not secrets regulation or storage management. `doctor --lint` reads config-level declarations, emits redacted findings, and leaves credential stores and runtime behavior to the existing auth/credential systems.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: config-level Policy conformance for redacted secret provenance and auth-profile metadata/mode rules.

Real environment tested: WSL checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-81974-policy` on Ubuntu-24.04; PR head `d72baa8115b12041a1c532479e919480c46eda57` is signed and GitHub-verified.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git push fork policy-81974-config:policy-secrets-auth-provenance`
- `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/policy-proof-81974/openclaw.json timeout 180 node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json --severity-min error`
- Earlier same-head verification: `pnpm exec oxfmt extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts`
- Earlier same-head verification: `git diff --check`
- Earlier same-head verification: `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- Earlier same-head verification: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts --run --reporter=dot`
- Earlier same-head verification: `node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test-policy-81974-original-lint.tsbuildinfo`
- Earlier same-head verification: autoreview local pass #1 and #2 clean, no accepted/actionable findings

Evidence after fix: the real `doctor --lint` CLI run loaded a temp workspace policy and config, ran 37 checks, and returned four redacted policy errors:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "checksRun": 37,
  "checksSkipped": 0,
  "findings": [
    {
      "checkId": "policy/auth-profile-unapproved-mode",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/auth/profiles/oauth",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/auth/profiles/allowModes",
      "message": "Auth profile 'oauth' uses mode 'oauth' outside the policy allowlist."
    },
    {
      "checkId": "policy/secrets-denied-provider-source",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/secrets/providers/command",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/secrets/denySources",
      "message": "Secret provider 'command' uses denied source 'exec'."
    },
    {
      "checkId": "policy/secrets-insecure-provider",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/secrets/providers/vault",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/secrets/allowInsecureProviders",
      "message": "Secret provider 'vault' enables insecure posture: allowInsecurePath."
    },
    {
      "checkId": "policy/secrets-unmanaged-provider",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/models/providers/anthropic/apiKey",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/secrets/requireManagedProviders",
      "message": "SecretRef uses unmanaged provider 'missing'."
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: the CLI reported the expected secret/auth conformance findings through `doctor --lint` without printing private secret values.

What was not tested: no live Gateway was launched; this is config-level policy conformance proof only.

## Related

- #80783 - Policy: add model, network, and MCP conformance checks
- #81981 - Policy: add gateway exposure checks

target.title: security: sanitize control characters in channel error logging
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `sanitizeForLog()` helper that strips C0 control characters (`\^@`–`\^_`) and DEL (`\u007f`) from values before they reach the log sink
- Apply sanitization to `error`, `target`, and `reason` parameters in `logTypingFailure`, `logAckFailure`, and `logInboundDrop`
- Add comprehensive test coverage for log injection scenarios

**Motivation:** Error objects and external metadata (targets, reasons) flow into structured log lines. A crafted error `toString()` containing `\n` or `\r` can forge additional log entries, potentially misleading operators or triggering false alerts in log-monitoring pipelines. Replacing control characters with spaces preserves the diagnostic value while preventing log forging.

## Test plan

- [x] New `src/channels/logging.test.ts` with 6 test cases covering:
  - Newline stripping in error messages
  - Carriage return stripping
  - NUL and other control characters in target parameter
  - Control characters in reason parameter
  - Malicious `toString()` on error objects
  - Normal messages pass through unmodified
- [x] `pnpm test -- src/channels/logging.test.ts` passes

target.title: fix(ios): stabilize chat streaming layout and session flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: iOS chat streaming could attach to a synthetic placeholder lane, churn during initial history/bootstrap, and reparse streaming content in ways that caused flicker or session drift.
- Why it matters: active conversations can show unstable streaming UI, lose the correct lane/session association, or visibly flicker while tokens arrive.
- What changed: scoped streaming to the active session key, hardened stream acceptance/initial load behavior, and stabilized layout parsing/visibility updates.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no gateway protocol changes, no new model behavior. This fix lives in the shared `OpenClawChatUI` layer used by both iOS and macOS. The bug (cross-run UI drift during concurrent session-key agent events) affects any client using `ChatViewModel`; macOS uses the same chat view and benefits equally, but the fix is not platform-specific — it guards shared streaming state.
- AI-assisted: yes (AI-assisted implementation, contributor-reviewed).
- Testing level: targeted shared-package tests plus iOS build validation.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #40985
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: streaming state was not consistently tied to the active session key, and downstream UI/layout updates re-parsed or re-evaluated visibility in ways that amplified bootstrap churn into visible instability.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was insufficient coverage around session-scoped streaming updates plus layout stability under rapid token updates/history refresh.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): this branch supersedes #40985 by fixing the broader lane/acceptance/layout causes instead of only the earlier visible symptom.
- Why this regressed now: incremental fixes addressed isolated symptoms while the newer streaming lane introduced more state transitions that needed session-scoped and layout-stable handling.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: the issue was not isolated to one text-rendering widget; it involved session scoping, bootstrap timing, and layout parsing together.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient

target.title: fix: exclude Markdown image syntax from bash bang detection
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Markdown image syntax `![image](url)` was misidentified as a bash bang command (`!command`) in TUI input handling and ACP dispatch, causing messages to enter the wrong processing pipeline.
- **Why it matters:** Users sending Markdown-formatted messages with images would trigger unintended shell confirmation or incorrect command routing.
- **What changed:** Added `![` prefix exclusion in 5 locations: `command-detection.ts` (regex), `bash-command.ts` (parser), `commands-bash.ts` (command layer), `tui.ts` (TUI input), and `dispatch-acp.ts` (ACP dispatch). Added corresponding tests covering multiple Markdown image variants.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No changes to actual bash bang execution logic or other command detection paths.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] UI / DX
- [x] Integrations

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Markdown image syntax (`![alt](url)`, `![](url)`, `![a]`) is no longer misrouted as a bash bang command in TUI or messaging channels.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No (narrowed — fewer false positives)
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Steps

1. Send `![image](https://example.com/img.png)` in TUI or via a messaging channel
2. Observe the message is treated as normal text, not a bash bang command

### Expected

Message passes through as normal text.

### Actual (before fix)

Message enters bash bang confirmation flow or ACP dispatch bang branch.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

Tests added:
- `commands.test.ts`: `it.each` covering 4 Markdown image variants
- `tui.submit-handler.test.ts`: 2 new tests for TUI input
- `command-control.test.ts`: 3 new assertions for `hasInlineCommandTokens`

All 3 test files pass (55/55, 13/13, 20/20 relevant tests).

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: all Markdown image variants (`![image](url)`, `![](url)`, `![a]`, `![image]`) across TUI, command detection, bash parsing, and ACP dispatch
- Edge cases checked: lone `!`, valid bang commands like `!ls`, mixed text with inline images
- What you did **not** verify: live end-to-end messaging channel test

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- Revert this single commit to restore previous behavior
- No config or data changes to undo

## Risks and Mitigations

None — pure string prefix check additions with no side effects.

target.title: fix(auth): persist final auth profile after successful fallback
target.body:
## Summary

- return `finalAuthProfileId` from embedded runs after successful fallback rotation
- persist the winning auth profile back to session `authProfileOverride`
- use the resolved winning profile id for persistence instead of a stale/raw profile source
- preserve `authProfileOverrideSource = "user"` when the session started from a user-pinned auth profile
- add focused regression coverage for final-profile persistence and user-pinned source preservation

## Why this replacement PR exists

This PR replaces #46642.

The earlier PR accumulated correct fixes, but its latest heads repeatedly failed to trigger the normal `pull_request` workflows (`CI`, `Install Smoke`, `Workflow Sanity`) and only ran `pull_request_target` labeler workflows, even after both empty and non-empty retrigger commits. To restore normal CI visibility, this replacement PR carries the same intended fix on a fresh branch.

## Problem

When `openai-codex:default` hit cooldown/rate-limit and the run successfully rotated to `openai-codex:account2`:

- `lastGood` could be updated
- but the session `authProfileOverride` could remain pinned to the stale default profile
- so the next request could start from the wrong/stale override again

There was also a source-tracking edge case where a user-pinned override source could be unintentionally rewritten as `auto` after a successful fallback rotation.

## Root cause

- the embedded runner did not reliably propagate the final winning auth profile id for session persistence
- successful fallback runs did not consistently persist the winner back into the session override
- the persistence path needed to preserve the original `user` source when a user-pinned session successfully rotated to another profile

## What changed

1. track the winning profile with the resolved final profile id
2. expose it as `finalAuthProfileId` in embedded run results
3. persist that id back to session `authProfileOverride` after a successful run
4. preserve `authProfileOverrideSource = "user"` when the session started user-pinned
5. add regression tests for:
   - final fallback winner persistence
   - first-run session persistence
   - user-pinned source preservation

## What this PR does NOT change

- it does not change initial auth-profile selection order logic (that stays in #45462)
- it does not change `lastGood` provider-key normalization persistence logic (that stays in #45699)
- it does not alter user intent by converting existing user-pinned sessions into automatic selection mode

## Validation

### Manual validation

1. set `lastGood = null`
2. put `openai-codex:default` in cooldown
3. run a request
4. verify fallback succeeds on `openai-codex:account2`
5. verify both:
   - `lastGood = openai-codex:account2`
   - `authProfileOverride = openai-codex:account2`
6. run a second request and confirm it stays on `account2`

### Regression coverage

- persists final rotated auth profile after a successful run
- allows first-run session persistence
- preserves user-pinned auth profile source when persisting the final rotated auth profile

## Scope

This PR is intentionally limited to successful fallback winner persistence. It is the runtime/session persistence leg of the auth-profile fallback fix set and is intentionally separate from #45462 and #45699.
