target.title: ACP: add gateway-owned node-backed runtime
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw did not yet have a complete **node-backed ACP runtime** where the gateway owns the durable state and a paired node acts only as the execution worker.
- Why it matters: without that split, restart/replay/reconnect behavior is too dependent on the live process, which makes lease fencing, terminal resolution, and outward delivery recovery harder to reason about and prove.
- What this PR adds:
  - a durable ACP gateway store for sessions, runs, accepted worker events, leases, terminal state, replay targets, and projector checkpoints
  - gateway ingestion of ACP worker events coming back from paired nodes
  - a real node-host ACP worker bridge and gateway/node command plumbing
  - a durable ACP reply projection/replay path so restart recovery can resume from gateway state instead of only from the live iterator
  - startup recovery for incomplete ACP node runs
  - restart/reconnect/cancel/close race hardening with focused restart/replay test coverage
  - architecture / protocol / verification docs for the new backend
- Landing-only follow-up: after rebasing onto current `main`, this PR also includes a few **test-only** isolation fixes in Feishu / Google / Google Chat extension tests that were needed to make the landing branch green. Those are not ACP runtime behavior changes.

## What changed by area

### 1) Durable ACP gateway state

New ACP store/state plumbing under `src/acp/store/**`:

- persists ACP sessions and runs
- persists accepted worker events
- tracks leases / lease epochs
- resolves canonical terminal state on the gateway
- stores replay targets and projector checkpoints separately from the runtime cursor

This is the foundation that lets the gateway stay authoritative even when execution happens on a paired node.

### 2) Gateway ↔ node ACP worker path

Main implementation areas:

- `src/gateway/**`
- `src/infra/node-commands.ts`
- `src/node-host/**`

This adds the node-backed ACP execution path:

- gateway can start/control ACP work on a paired node
- node-host can run the ACP worker role
- worker events stream back to the gateway over the existing node transport
- gateway remains the source of truth for run state and terminal resolution

### 3) Durable reply projection and replay

Main implementation areas:

- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-replay.ts`

This PR moves ACP outward delivery onto a durable replayable path:

- reply targets are persisted per run
- projector checkpoints are advanced only after successful delivery
- restart recovery can replay the unfinished suffix from durable state
- reconnect and duplicate accepted events do not re-deliver already completed outward effects

### 4) Startup recovery / reconnect handling

Main implementation areas:

- `src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.ts`
- `src/gateway/server-node-events.ts`
- `src/acp/runtime/acp-node.ts`

This adds the gateway-side recovery flow needed to:

- resume incomplete projection after restart
- keep reconnect handling deterministic
- prevent stale-worker wins
- preserve canonical terminal behavior across restart/replay windows

### 5) Test harnesses and regression coverage

Main test additions/expansions:

- `src/acp/store/store.restart.test.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.replay.test.ts`
- `src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.test.ts`
- `src/gateway/server-node-events.acp.test.ts`
- `src/node-host/invoke-acp.test.ts`
- `src/acp/test-harness/restart-harness.ts`

These cover the core failure windows this backend needs to survive:

- restart after accepted events but before replay checkpoint advancement
- restart after terminal persistence but before final outward convergence
- reconnect replay of already accepted events
- cancel / complete / close race handling
- restart recovery with a real memory cut instead of reusing in-process objects

### 6) Docs included in this PR

Main docs:

- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime-protocol.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime-verification.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-runtime-program.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-slice4-summary.md`
- `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-slice4-final-judge.md`

These are included because this was developed as a staged architecture / verification effort and the final accepted behavior is documented there.

## Explicit scope boundaries / v1 limitations

This PR does **not**:

- turn nodes into mini-gateways
- add automatic cross-node continuation for an in-flight run
- claim durable resumed tool-message edit support after restart

Current conservative post-restart tool-update behavior remains:

- `append_only_after_restart`

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related: ACP node runtime architecture / protocol / verification docs under `docs/experiments/plans/`
- Related: landing-only extension test isolation fixes after rebase onto current `main`

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- OpenClaw now has a first-class ACP node-backed runtime path where the gateway owns durable ACP truth and paired nodes execute the worker role.
- Gateway restart/recovery can resume incomplete ACP outward projection from durable state.
- Duplicate accepted worker-event replay on reconnect is hardened so it does not duplicate outward delivery.
- Post-restart tool updates intentionally follow the documented conservative v1 rule: `append_only_after_restart`.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes
- Data access scope changed? Yes

### Security / trust-boundary notes

- Paired nodes can now advertise ACP worker capability and receive ACP runtime commands over the existing gateway↔node trust boundary.
- Gateway↔node websocket traffic now carries ACP worker control / event flows in addition to existing node commands.
- Gateway now persists ACP runtime state needed for durable replay/recovery.
- The design mitigates stale-worker / duplicate-delivery risks via lease epochs, canonical terminal resolution, durable replay checkpoints, and reconnect/replay handling on the gateway.

## Repro + Verification

- Runtime/container: Node / pnpm; Bun build path also checked
- Model/provider: N/A

### Verified locally

  - `pnpm test -- src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.replay.test.ts src/acp/store/store.test.ts src/acp/store/store.restart.test.ts src/acp/store/gateway-events.test.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.acp.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.test.ts src/node-host/invoke-acp.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/nodes.acp.test.ts`
- TypeScript guardrail:

- Landing-only extension test fixes:
  - `pnpm test:extensions`

### CI / build notes

- `pnpm build` still fails only with the pre-existing Bun wrapper issue:
  - `Cannot find module './cjs/index.cjs' from ''`
- That build failure was already present and is not introduced by the ACP runtime changes in this PR.

## Evidence

- [x] Trace/log snippets

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified the ACP-focused local gate listed above.

What I did **not** verify:

## Review Guide

If you want the shortest path through this PR, review in this order:

1. **Core architecture / intent**
   - `docs/experiments/plans/acp-node-backed-runtime.md`

5. **Recovery and proofs**
   - `src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.ts`
   - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.replay.test.ts`
   - `src/gateway/server-startup.acp-node.test.ts`
6. **Ignore as landing-only test fixes unless you are checking CI fallout**

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR or avoid selecting/using the `acp-node` runtime path until fixed.
- Files/config to restore: ACP runtime/store/gateway/node-host changes under `src/acp/**`, `src/node-host/**`, `src/gateway/**`, plus the accompanying docs if reverting wholesale.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: duplicate final delivery after restart, stale worker winning after reconnect, projector checkpoint drift, or replay targeting the wrong route.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: restart/replay correctness is subtle in any durable projection system.
  - Mitigation: this PR includes restart harnesses, startup recovery tests, reconnect/replay ordering tests, and explicit protocol/verification docs.
- Risk: the PR is large and spans multiple ACP runtime layers.
  - Mitigation: the review guide above splits it into durable store, node bridge, replay path, recovery path, and landing-only CI fixes.
- Risk: paired-node ACP execution expands the gateway↔node command surface.
  - Mitigation: the gateway remains authoritative for leases, terminal resolution, checkpoints, and replay instead of trusting node-local completion.

commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a no-arg `/codex` picker, menu-style Codex subcommands, Telegram `cdx_*` in-place callback routing, dynamic plugin enable/disable pickers, and focused Telegram picker tests on top of the mainline `/codex plugins list|enable|disable` commands.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection shows `/codex plugins menu` is advertised without a handler, and the picker computes plugin state without the global `codexPlugins.enabled` flag. The original request is a feature addition, not a current-main bug reproduction.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor fixes plus current-head real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not take over while the proof gate is stale and the no-arg `/codex` UX change needs maintainer acceptance.

target.title: feat(codex): chat surface for managing Codex sub-plugins, permissions, and account, with in-place picker UX
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: every Codex sub-plugin and every Codex setting that an Openclaw user might want to flip lives inside `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`. There is no slash command, no picker, and no Telegram surface for the most common Codex operations: enabling and disabling a sub-plugin, switching the approval policy, checking which Codex account is signed in. Telegram and Discord users who run openclaw the most never see openclaw.json at all. The friction goes up the moment Codex migration adds or renames a sub-plugin.
- Why it matters: this is the most common day-to-day question for active Codex users ("is computer-use on right now, do I need to restart, how do I flip yolo for one task"). Today the only answer is "open your editor and find the right block of JSON".
- What changed: adds a `/codex` chat surface owned by `@openclaw/codex` plus the Telegram in-place-edit picker that drives it.
  - New `/codex plugins` subcommand with verbs `list`, `enable`, `disable`, `toggle`, `add`, `remove`, `help`. When a verb that needs a plugin name is called with no name, returns a picker (text fallback plus interactive button block).
  - The top-level `/codex` picker is redesigned to a 4-button menu in a 2x2 grid (plugins, permissions, account, help), down from the previous wall of 14 buttons that duplicated openclaw-level commands.
  - Buttons edit the message in place via new `cdx_*` callback handlers, mirroring the existing `/model` Browse providers pattern. Back buttons on every sub-menu, parent-aware routing.
  - Dynamic plugin pickers read the live `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` so new plugins surfaced by Codex migration show up automatically.
  - `RESTART_HINT` renamed to `POLICY_REFRESH_HINT` with corrected text: "New Codex conversations pick this up automatically. Use /new or /reset to refresh the current one."
  - Includes a small fix to `ensureCodexPluginActivation` so the missing-marketplace check uses the actual marketplace from the activation identity instead of a hardcoded curated reference (uncovered while wiring up the picker).
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no Codex app-server behavior touched beyond the marketplace-check fix. No new dependency, no new env var, no new public config key. The chat surface reads and writes the same `plugins.entries.codex.config.codexPlugins.plugins` block that Codex migration and the existing config resolver already use. The 4-button picker drops verbs that already exist at the openclaw level (`/status`, `/help`, `/stop`, `/models`, `/fast`) from the button list; they remain typeable.

## Change Type

- [x] Feature
- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Integrations
- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #82218
- Related #82219 (data-layer fix that widens the marketplace allowlist so the picker can list and manage bundled and primary-runtime plugins; first commit on this branch is a stacked copy of #82219 and will drop out on rebase once #82219 merges)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression (the marketplace-check fix in `ensureCodexPluginActivation`)

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex sub-plugin and Codex setting management is impossible from chat today. After this patch, `/codex` opens a 4-button picker (plugins, permissions, account, help), each button edits the message in place to drill into its sub-menu, every sub-menu has a back button, and the plugins sub-tree exposes list, enable, disable, toggle, add, remove via typed verb or picker.
- Real environment tested: macOS 15.4 Apple Silicon, Openclaw 2026.5.12 managed install at `/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw`, `@openclaw/codex` plugin enabled, Mac Gateway running under `openclaw gateway`, Telegram channel bound to `agent:main`. Codex configured with three plugins across the three OpenAI marketplaces (curated, bundled, primary-runtime) so the picker has a realistic surface.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `pnpm build`.
  2. `pnpm tsgo:extensions` and `npx oxfmt --check extensions/codex extensions/telegram` for type and format proof.
  3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/plugin-activation.test.ts` to verify the marketplace-check fix.
  4. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/codex-picker-buttons.test.ts extensions/codex/src/command-plugins-management.test.ts extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts` for picker and subcommand tests.
  5. Hot-patched the picker dist into the live install (so chat-side smoke testing did not require a fresh release build) and ran in Telegram: `/codex` (4-button picker appears), tapped plugins (in-place edit into plugins menu), tapped enable (in-place edit into dynamic plugin picker showing only disabled plugins), tapped a plugin name (mutates openclaw.json, replies with policy-refresh hint, message edits back to plugins menu), tapped back (returns to top picker), tapped permissions (sub-menu opens in place), tapped help (Codex-scoped help). Then ran `/codex plugins list`, `/codex plugins disable chrome`, `/codex plugins enable chrome`, `/codex plugins toggle documents`, `/codex plugins disable` (no name, picker fallback). Inspected `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` after each mutation to confirm persisted state matches what chat reported.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture and live output):

  Targeted unit tests pass after the marketplace fix is in place:

  ```
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/plugin-activation.test.ts
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  9 passed (9)
     Duration  ~350ms

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/codex-picker-buttons.test.ts \
                                 extensions/codex/src/command-plugins-management.test.ts \
                                 extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts
   Test Files  3 passed (3)
        Tests  118 passed (118)
     Duration  ~6.7s
  ```

  Live `/codex plugins list` from Telegram, with three plugins configured (curated, bundled, primary-runtime):

  ```
  Codex sub-plugins in Openclaw config (~/.openclaw/openclaw.json):

    ON   chrome           chrome           [openai-bundled]
    ON   documents        documents        [openai-primary-runtime]
    ON   google-calendar  google-calendar  [openai-curated]

  Run 'openclaw gateway restart' to load any policy changes.
  ```

  Live `/codex plugins disable chrome` from Telegram:

  ```
  chrome: disabled in openclaw.json. New Codex conversations pick this up automatically. Use /new or /reset to refresh the current one.
  ```

  `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` after that command shows `"chrome": { "enabled": false, "marketplaceName": "openai-bundled", "pluginName": "chrome" }`.

  In-place picker edit: tapping the plugins button on the top `/codex` picker replaces the message body in place with the plugins menu (no second message posted), via the `cdx_plugins_menu` callback. Tapping back inside any sub-menu replaces the body again with the previous picker, parent-aware. The dynamic plugin picker reads the live `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` so the same enable/disable surface adapts as Codex migration adds or renames plugins.

  Format and types clean on the touched files (`pnpm tsgo:extensions` shows no codex errors; only two pre-existing Discord errors in `extensions/discord/src/monitor/inbound-context.ts:65,68` that are present on `upstream/main` too and are not introduced by this PR).

- Observed result after fix: every verb works end-to-end. Every button on the 4-button picker drives an in-place edit, mirrors the `/model` Browse providers UX. Mutations land in `openclaw.json` atomically. The picker fallback works on Telegram with buttons and on channels without interactive support via the text fallback. Policy-refresh hint nudges the operator to use `/new` or `/reset` instead of restarting the gateway, which matches Codex's actual hot-reload story.
- What was not tested: did not test on Discord (no test account on this install). Did not run the full repo suite locally; narrow proof scoped to `extensions/codex` and `extensions/telegram`. CI runs the full gate on push. There is one pre-existing failure in `extensions/codex/src/migration/provider.test.ts > leaves selected Codex plugins as warnings when target curated plugins never load` that also fails on `upstream/main` (expected `plugin_missing`, got `marketplace_missing`); confirmed reproducible on plain main, not introduced by this PR.
- Before evidence: on `upstream/main`, `/codex plugins` returns "Unknown subcommand: plugins". The top-level `/codex` picker shows 14 buttons, several of which duplicate openclaw-level commands. There is no in-place edit; every tap posts a fresh message. The Codex sub-plugin set can only be inspected by `cat`ing `openclaw.json`, and changed only by editing that file.

## Root Cause

The marketplace-check fix (`fix(codex): use identity marketplace name in ensureCodexPluginActivation missing-marketplace check`) addresses a real bug introduced when the marketplace allowlist widened from a single value to an array. The original code referenced the singular `CODEX_PLUGINS_MARKETPLACE_NAME` constant in the missing-marketplace check after the constant was renamed; the result was a `ReferenceError` when activation hit the failure branch (caught downstream as a generic plugin-install error). Replacing the hardcoded curated check with `params.identity.marketplaceName` is semantically correct for the wider allowlist: the missing-marketplace check should ask "is the marketplace this plugin claims to come from actually loaded" rather than "is the curated marketplace loaded".

The chat surface itself is feature-add, no underlying bug; the operator pain is documented in #82218.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `extensions/codex/src/command-plugins-management.test.ts`, `extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/codex-picker-buttons.test.ts`, and the existing `extensions/codex/src/app-server/plugin-activation.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - Each `/codex plugins` verb routes correctly, mutations land in the injected IO interface, picker fallback returns both text and a `MessagePresentation` button block, picker filters by state (enable shows disabled, disable shows enabled, toggle and remove show all).
  - The top picker payload returns exactly 4 buttons in 2x2 grid.
  - The cdx_* callback parser routes top, plugins-menu, permissions-menu, fast-menu, plugin-name pickers, and back buttons to the correct parent.
  - The marketplace-check fix path: when target marketplaces is empty, activation returns `marketplace_missing` for any identity marketplace, no `ReferenceError`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the picker handlers are pure functions over injected dependencies (a `CodexPluginsManagementIO` interface for the config read/write and a runtime helper for the live plugin list), so they can be tested with in-memory fakes. The cdx_* callback parser is a pure router. No filesystem, no gateway, no Codex app-server required.
- Existing test that already covers this: none. The slash command did not exist before this PR, the picker did not exist before this PR, and the marketplace-check bug was not exercised by the pre-existing `plugin-activation.test.ts` happy-path tests.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

A new `/codex plugins` verb is available on every channel where the Codex plugin is loaded. Calling `/codex` with no subverb now shows a 4-button picker (plugins, permissions, account, help) instead of the previous 14-button layout. Existing typeable verbs (`status`, `threads`, `bind`, `model`, `fast`, `permissions`, `account`, `help`, `computer-use`, etc) all keep working exactly as they did before. No defaults change.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
  user types /codex
    -> reply with 14 buttons (plugins missing; status, fast, help, stop, models duplicating openclaw commands)
    -> tapping any button posts a fresh message
  user wants to disable chrome
    -> open ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json in an editor
    -> find plugins.entries.codex.config.codexPlugins.plugins.chrome
    -> set "enabled": false
    -> save, run openclaw restart
    -> hope you got the path right

After:
  user types /codex
    -> reply with 4 buttons in 2x2: plugins | permissions
                                    account | help
    -> tap plugins button (cdx_plugins_menu)
       -> message edits in place to plugins sub-menu
       -> tap disable
          -> message edits in place to dynamic plugin picker (enabled plugins only)
          -> tap chrome
             -> mutate openclaw.json atomically
             -> reply with policy-refresh hint
             -> message edits back to plugins menu
          -> tap back at any depth
             -> message edits to parent picker
  user can also type any verb directly:
    /codex plugins list / enable / disable / toggle / add / remove / help
    /codex permissions default / yolo
    /codex account
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No. The new verb writes to the same `openclaw.json` block that operators already write by hand.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No. The verb is local read and write of `openclaw.json`. The picker callbacks are local message edits via the existing Telegram bot API surface.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes, additively: a new chat verb (`/codex plugins`) and a redesigned chat picker. Neither can do anything an operator could not already do by editing the config file manually.
- Data access scope changed? No.
- Risk and mitigation: the only material change is "chat can mutate `openclaw.json`". This is gated by the standard plugin command authorization that every `/codex` verb already uses. Picker callbacks (cdx_* prefix) are authorized via the same callback_query handler that gates the existing model_* and other plugin pickers.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15.4 (Apple Silicon)
- Runtime/container: Node 22, Openclaw 2026.5.12 managed install (`/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw`)
- Model/provider: `gpt-5.4` via `agentRuntime.id=codex`
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram, Mac Gateway
- Relevant config (redacted): three plugins under `plugins.entries.codex.config.codexPlugins.plugins`, one per OpenAI marketplace (curated, bundled, primary-runtime). Codex CLI auth under `~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json`.

### Steps

1. On `upstream/main`, run `/codex` in Telegram. Observe the 14-button picker. Run `/codex plugins`. Observe "Unknown subcommand: plugins".
2. Apply this PR's patch. Rebuild.
3. Run `/codex` in Telegram. Observe the 4-button picker (plugins, permissions, account, help) in 2x2 grid.
4. Tap plugins. Observe message edits in place to the plugins sub-menu.
5. Tap disable. Observe dynamic picker listing only currently-enabled plugins.
6. Tap chrome. Observe `openclaw.json` mutation and policy-refresh reply.
7. Tap back. Observe parent picker returns.
8. Type `/codex plugins list`, `/codex plugins enable chrome`, `/codex plugins toggle documents`, `/codex plugins disable` (no name), `/codex plugins help`.
9. Compare `openclaw.json` before and after each mutation.

### Expected

Every picker tap edits the active message in place. Every typed verb returns a clean reply plus, where applicable, a button block. `openclaw.json` reflects each mutation atomically. Policy-refresh hint mentions `/new` and `/reset`, not gateway restart.

### Actual

Matches expected.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before plus passing after (new picker and subcommand tests fail on `upstream/main` because the modules do not exist there, and pass on this branch)
- [x] Trace/log snippets (chat replies and tests shown above)

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: every verb on Telegram (list, enable, disable, toggle, add, remove, help). Top picker (4 buttons, 2x2). Every sub-menu in-place edit. Back navigation from every depth. Dynamic plugin picker reads live `openclaw.json`. Policy-refresh hint text. Marketplace fix exercised via plugin-activation tests.
- Edge cases checked: `enable` on a plugin that is not configured returns a useful error. `disable` on an already-disabled plugin is a no-op. `add` with an unknown marketplace name is rejected with a clear error pointing at the three allowed values. Empty plugin list returns an empty-state message. Picker callback with malformed payload is ignored cleanly.
- What I did **not** verify: Discord channel (no test account on this install). Mac Gateway hot-reload without a full restart. Cross-platform behavior on Windows or Linux.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes. The new picker is the default for `/codex` with no args; the previous 14-button picker is gone. Every typeable verb still works exactly the same. The new `/codex plugins` verb is additive.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No. Operators with existing `plugins.entries.codex.config.codexPlugins.plugins` blocks pick this up automatically.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: dropping verbs from the picker (status, fast, help, stop, models) could surprise an operator who was relying on them.
  - Mitigation: every dropped verb stays typeable. The dropped verbs are either duplicates of openclaw-level commands (status, help, stop) or rarely-used (fast, models).
- Risk: the dynamic plugin picker depends on the marketplace allowlist being widened (#82219); without it, bundled and primary-runtime plugins are dropped at config-read time and never appear in the picker.
  - Mitigation: the marketplace fix is the first commit on this branch (stacked from #82219) and will drop out cleanly on rebase once #82219 merges. The picker functionality degrades gracefully if the allowlist is narrow: it just lists fewer plugins.
- Risk: in-place edit could feel jarring if the user expects a fresh reply.
  - Mitigation: matches the existing `/model` Browse providers pattern, which is the established precedent in this codebase for cross-extension UI ownership. Telegram users already trained on that surface.

target.title: fix(gateway): close child ACP sessions on parent reset/delete (#68916)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: ACP sessions spawned from a parent session via `sessions_spawn` (e.g. `runtime: "acp"`) leave their `claude-agent-acp` + `claude` child processes alive after the parent session is reset (`/new`, `/reset`) or deleted. The gateway session-mutation cleanup in `src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts` (`cleanupSessionBeforeMutation`) only closes the ACP runtime for the parent's own session key via `closeAcpRuntimeForSession`; it never enumerates and closes the child ACP sessions spawned from it. The orphaned pairs accumulate and exhaust memory (~3.5 GB from 11 orphaned pairs within 2 hours on a 16 GB host).
- **Root Cause**: Child ACP sessions are persisted as their own session-store entries that record their requester's canonical session key. ACP spawns (`sessions_spawn` with `runtime: "acp"`) record it in `spawnedBy` (see `acp-spawn.ts` → `sessions.patch({ spawnedBy })`), while subagent spawns use `parentSessionKey`. Two compounding gaps left them orphaned: (1) the cleanup matched only `parentSessionKey`, missing ACP children that record lineage in `spawnedBy`; and (2) the ACP child key is `agent:<targetAgentId>:acp:<uuid>`, so under the default per-agent store layout the child entry lives in the **target agent's** store — a different file from the parent's — and the cleanup only read the parent's own store. The reset/delete path therefore never cancelled/closed the spawned ACP runtimes, and their backend processes were orphaned (reparented to the supervisor).
- **Fix**: Add a bounded child-cleanup step to `cleanupSessionBeforeMutation`. After the parent's ACP runtime is closed, `closeChildAcpRuntimesForParent` enumerates **all agent stores** via the existing `loadCombinedSessionStoreForGateway` (the same combined cross-agent store the dashboard `sessions.list` uses, which resolves per-agent stores under canonical keys), then selects direct ACP-backed children by the real spawn lineage — `(entry.spawnedBy === parentKey || entry.parentSessionKey === parentKey) && entry.acp` — matching how ACP and subagent spawns record their requester (mirrors `buildStoreChildSessionIndex`). It closes them through the existing `closeAcpRuntimeForSession` helper, which resolves each child's own store from its key (reusing the established cancel/close/discard behavior). Both `spawnedBy` and `parentKey` are canonical session keys, so direct equality is correct. Children are closed **concurrently** via `Promise.allSettled`, so total cleanup latency is bounded by a single ACP cleanup timeout window rather than scaling with the number of stuck children (each close has a 15s cancel/close timeout; a serial drain would stall reset/delete by N × 15s). Per-child failures are logged best-effort and never propagated, and the parent's own close result is returned unchanged. This reuses the proven ACP teardown path rather than introducing a new one. Only direct children are enumerated; grandchild ACP sessions are out of scope for this fix.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts`: enumerate children via `loadCombinedSessionStoreForGateway` (all agent stores) instead of the parent store only; add `closeChildAcpRuntimesForParent` helper matching `spawnedBy || parentSessionKey` lineage; call it from `cleanupSessionBeforeMutation` after the parent close, preserving the parent close result as the return value.
  - `src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-cleanup.test.ts`: regression tests that `sessions.reset` closes spawned ACP children linked via `spawnedBy` and `parentSessionKey`, a cross-store test where the child lives in a different agent store, and a concurrency test proving stuck children are dispatched together (not serialized).
  - `src/gateway/server.sessions.delete-lifecycle.test.ts`: a parallel test that `sessions.delete` closes a `spawnedBy`-linked ACP child of the deleted parent.
  - `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line `### Fixes` entry.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - `closeAcpRuntimeForSession`, `cancelSession`, `closeSession`, and the parent close result/return contract are unchanged — children reuse the identical close path.
  - No PID-level fallback kill and no global/startup orphan reaping were added; per the issue triage those remain separate follow-ups. This fix addresses the specific parent-reset/delete enumeration gap only.
  - No new config keys, no change to `sessions_spawn`, ACP runtime backends, or the session-store schema.
  - Non-ACP child sessions and unrelated sessions are untouched (filtered out by `entry.acp` and the lineage match).

### Reproduction

1. From a main session, spawn several ACP runs: `sessions_spawn({ runtime: "acp", mode: "run", task: "..." })`.
2. Reset the parent session via `/new` or `/reset` (or delete it).
3. Before the fix: every spawned `claude-agent-acp` + `claude` pair stays alive (reparented to the supervisor) and memory grows over time. After the fix: each spawned ACP child is cancelled/closed alongside the parent.

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: on parent session reset/delete, spawned child ACP sessions were never enumerated, so their ACP runtimes (and backend processes) were orphaned; only the parent's own ACP runtime was closed.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux, Node.js 22, real on-disk **per-agent** session stores (`agents/<id>/sessions/sessions.json`) written to a temp state dir and read back through the real `loadCombinedSessionStoreForGateway` (the cross-agent aggregator the dashboard uses) — stores are not stubbed.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: (A)+(B) wrote a parent ACP session to the `main` agent store and, in a **different** agent store (`codex`), two ACP children linked via `spawnedBy` (the field `sessions_spawn`'s ACP path writes), one subagent-style child linked via `parentSessionKey`, one non-ACP child, and one unrelated ACP session; then compared the old path (read parent store + `parentSessionKey` only) against the fix (combined cross-agent store + `spawnedBy || parentSessionKey`) to count which ACP children the reset/delete path closes; (C) measured close latency by simulating three stuck child closes (200ms each) under a serial `await` loop (old) versus `Promise.allSettled` (fix).
- **Evidence after fix**: console output from the run:
  ```
  (A)+(B) cross-store discovery + lineage (parent=agent:main:main, children in codex store)
    OLD (parent store + parentSessionKey only): closes 0 child -> 3 ACP children ORPHANED
    FIX (combined store + spawnedBy|parent)    : closes 3 children -> 0 orphaned
    matched: agent:codex:acp:c1, agent:codex:acp:c2, agent:codex:sub:c3
  (C) latency for N=3 stuck children @ 200ms each
    serial await                   : ~603ms (~N × window)
    Promise.allSettled (fix)       : ~201ms (~one window)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: (A)+(B) the old path reads only the parent's store and matches only `parentSessionKey`, so it closes **none** of the three ACP children that live in the `codex` store — all orphaned; the fix enumerates the combined cross-agent store and matches `spawnedBy || parentSessionKey`, closing all three while excluding the non-ACP child and the unrelated ACP session. (C) closing three stuck children concurrently completes in ~one timeout window (~201ms) instead of ~N windows (~603ms serial). Gateway tests independently confirm `sessions.reset` closes a child in a **different agent store**, `sessions.reset`/`sessions.delete` close `spawnedBy`-linked children, and stuck children are dispatched concurrently — all fail on current `main` without this change.
- **What was not tested**: a live multi-hour gateway run reproducing the full ~3.5 GB memory growth and OS-level process reaping was not performed; the proof exercises the enumeration/selection that drives which ACP runtimes get closed rather than measuring host RSS end to end.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: a child cancel/close failure could stall or block the parent mutation, or the enumeration could close the wrong sessions.
- **Mitigation**: children are closed **concurrently** (`Promise.allSettled`), so a stuck child cannot serialize cleanup — total latency stays within one ACP cleanup timeout window instead of N × 15s. Per-child timeouts/errors are logged and never propagated, and the parent's own close result is returned unchanged, so parent-reset/delete behavior is preserved. Selection is strictly `entry.parentSessionKey === parentKey && entry.acp`, excluding the parent itself, non-ACP children, and unrelated sessions (verified by the proof's negative controls). The child path reuses the existing `closeAcpRuntimeForSession` helper, so cancel/close/discard semantics are identical to the parent. Reset, delete, and concurrency are covered by gateway regression tests (each fails on `main` without the fix); existing reset/subagent suites pass; prod typecheck (`tsgo:core`), `oxfmt`, and `oxlint` are clean; no `any` types introduced.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway
- [x] ACP subsystem
- [x] Tests

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #68916

target.title: feat: expose rawBody on user messages in plugin hook events
target.body:
### Summary

Add `rawBody` field to user messages in `agent_end`, `before_prompt_build`, and `before_agent_start` (legacy) hook events. This provides plugins with the original user input before channel structural context, `buildInboundUserContextPrefix()` metadata, and prompt-build hook injections are prepended.

### Motivation

Plugin hooks receive `event.messages` where user message `content` includes OpenClaw-injected metadata (timestamps, channel info, sender IDs) and other hook injections (e.g., memory context). Plugins that process user content for purposes like memory extraction, summarization, or analytics must parse and strip this metadata — a fragile approach since the format varies by channel, chat type, and OpenClaw version.

PR #51502 already established the precedent of using `RawBody` instead of `Body` to avoid passing envelope metadata to LLM processing. This PR extends that principle to plugin hook events.

### Changes

- Compute `rawBodyForHooks` from `CommandBody ?? RawBody ?? Body` in the channel message handler — the clean user text without @mentions, sender labels, or message annotations
- Thread `rawBody` through the run params chain: `get-reply-run.ts` → `runReplyAgent` → `runAgentTurnWithFallback` → `runEmbeddedPiAgent` → `runEmbeddedAttempt`
- Include `rawBody` in hook event objects for `before_prompt_build`, `before_agent_start`, and `agent_end`
- Add `rawBody` to `FollowupRun` type so the value survives queueing/deferred runs
- `rawBody` is `undefined` for non-channel runs (CLI, heartbeat, cron)

### Backward Compatibility

- Additive optional field only — no breaking changes
- Plugins can feature-detect `rawBody` and fall back to `content`:
  ```typescript
  const userText = event.rawBody ?? extractText(lastUser.content);
  ```
- Plugin SDK API baseline unchanged (no drift)

### Use Case

Memory plugins send user+assistant pairs to backend for memory extraction. Without `rawBody`, the plugin must regex-strip injected XML blocks and cannot remove inbound metadata, causing noisy "memories" from message IDs and timestamps. With `rawBody`, the plugin gets clean user input directly.

target.title: fix(clawdock): load docker-compose.override.yml
target.body:
Fixes #49909.

## Summary
- Load `docker-compose.override.yml` when ClawDock helpers invoke Docker Compose with explicit `-f` args.
- Keep `docker-compose.extra.yml` loaded after the standard override and document the order.
- Add a regression test for `_clawdock_compose` argument construction.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run test/scripts/clawdock-helpers.test.ts`
- `bash -n scripts/clawdock/clawdock-helpers.sh`
- `zsh -n scripts/clawdock/clawdock-helpers.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: ClawDock commands now include `docker-compose.override.yml` when present instead of silently ignoring it after explicit `-f` args disable Compose's default auto-merge.
Real environment tested: macOS Darwin arm64 shell using official Docker Compose standalone v5.1.3 for `config` resolution, through the ClawDock helper's `docker compose` invocation path.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CLAWDOCK_DIR=/private/tmp/openclaw-clawdock-real-proof-85313/project HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-clawdock-real-proof-85313/home PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw-clawdock-real-proof-85313/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin bash -lc 'source scripts/clawdock/clawdock-helpers.sh; _clawdock_compose config --no-interpolate'`
Evidence after fix: Real Docker Compose config output after the helper loaded `docker-compose.yml`, `docker-compose.override.yml`, and `docker-compose.extra.yml`:

```yaml
name: project
networks:
  default:
    name: project_default
services:
  proof:
    environment:
      - EXTRA_PRECEDENCE=extra
      - OPENCLAW_COMPOSE_LAYER=override
      - OVERRIDE_FILE_ACTIVE=yes
      - EXTRA_FILE_ACTIVE=yes
    image: alpine:3.20
    networks:
      default: null
```

Observed result after fix: `OVERRIDE_FILE_ACTIVE=yes` appears in the resolved Compose config, proving the standard override file is no longer ignored; `EXTRA_PRECEDENCE=extra` appears while `OPENCLAW_COMPOSE_LAYER=override` remains, proving `docker-compose.extra.yml` is loaded after the standard override and has final precedence for overlapping keys.
What was not tested: `docker compose up` against a running Docker daemon; this proof exercises Compose file resolution with `config`, which is the behavior changed by this patch.

target.title: feat(matrix): opt-in mention bypass for in-thread replies (threadBindings.bypassMentionInBoundThreads)
target.body:
Fixes #85111.

## Summary

Adds an opt-in `channels.matrix.threadBindings.bypassMentionInBoundThreads` (boolean, default false). When true, the matrix inbound handler skips the room-level `requireMention` gate for events that are replying inside a thread the bot already has prior activity in.

This enables the common bot UX of "mention to open a thread, free-form conversation continues inside it" on `requireMention` rooms — currently impossible because every in-thread reply that omits the mention is dropped as `skipping room message: no-mention`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: The matrix plugin's inbound handler drops in-thread replies in `requireMention: true` rooms even when the bot has been actively conversing in that thread. Users have to `@bot` on every line inside threads the bot opened, breaking the natural "mention to start, free-form continuation" UX. With this PR's opt-in flag, in-thread replies bypass the mention gate when the bot has a recorded session for the thread (via `recordInboundSession`'s thread-suffixed key) OR an explicit runtime binding (via `/focus`, `/acp bind`, etc.). Top-level messages keep `requireMention` enforcement; rooms without the flag set behave byte-identically to current `main`.

- **Real environment tested**: Unraid 7.2.5 host, `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:latest` (2026.5.19), Synapse via `ghcr.io/junkerderprovinz/matrix:latest` (AIO image), both containers on the same Docker bridge, Synapse Tailscale-served as `matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net`. Test room `parents` (`!pgXEKemZcQTfeiaNvM:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net`) configured under `channels.matrix.groups[<roomId>] = { requireMention: true, skills: [actual-budget, diagram-maker, news-summary] }`, three members (`@paulmeier`, `@openclaw`, `@admin`), E2EE on, `threadReplies: \"always\"`, `threadBindings: { enabled: true, idleHours: 24, spawnSessions: true, defaultSpawnContext: \"fork\" }`. The patched matrix monitor bundle (`/root/.openclaw/extensions/matrix/dist/monitor-4YYdKe_C.js`) was overlaid onto the running container via `docker cp`, then OpenClaw restarted. The unpatched bundle was preserved at `monitor-4YYdKe_C.js.bak` for revert.

- **Exact steps or command run after the patch**: Live-environment verification — overlay the patched matrix monitor bundle onto the running container, restart, then exercise the in-thread reply path in `#parents`:

  ```
  $ ssh root@<unraid> 'docker cp OpenClaw:/root/.openclaw/extensions/matrix/dist/monitor-4YYdKe_C.js /tmp/monitor-orig.js'
  $ scp root@<unraid>:/tmp/monitor-orig.js /tmp/monitor-orig.js
  $ python3 -c \"src=open('/tmp/monitor-orig.js').read();
    old='const shouldBypassMention = allowTextCommands && isRoom && shouldRequireMention && !wasMentioned && !hasExplicitMention && commandAuthorized && hasControlCommandInMessage;';
    new='const inThreadReply = threadRootId !== undefined && threadRootId !== messageId; let threadSessionExists = false; if (inThreadReply) { try { const _storePath = core.channel.session.resolveStorePath(cfg.session?.store, { agentId: _route.agentId }); const _ts = core.channel.session.readSessionUpdatedAt({ storePath: _storePath, sessionKey: _route.sessionKey }); threadSessionExists = _ts !== undefined && _ts !== null; } catch (_e) { threadSessionExists = false; } } const inExistingBoundThread = inThreadReply && (threadSessionExists || _runtimeBindingId !== null); const shouldBypassMention = isRoom && shouldRequireMention && !wasMentioned && !hasExplicitMention && ((allowTextCommands && commandAuthorized && hasControlCommandInMessage) || inExistingBoundThread);';
    open('/tmp/monitor-patched.js','w').write(src.replace(old, new, 1))\"
  $ scp /tmp/monitor-patched.js root@<unraid>:/tmp/
  $ ssh root@<unraid> 'docker cp /tmp/monitor-patched.js OpenClaw:/root/.openclaw/extensions/matrix/dist/monitor-4YYdKe_C.js && docker restart OpenClaw'
  ```

  In `#parents`, posted `@openclaw ping` (top-level mention; opens a thread), then in that thread posted `hello there` (no mention).

  Runtime acceptance-criteria suite — runtime executed locally against the patched branch (`308da8c5`):

  ```
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \\
      extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-room.test.ts \\
      extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-management.test.ts \\
      extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/direct.test.ts \\
      extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/handler.test.ts \\
      extensions/matrix/src/matrix/send/targets.test.ts
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**: Live patched-runtime gateway log on the deployment, redacted — bot processing the in-thread reply that was previously dropped. After the user's mention of \`@openclaw\` opens the thread, the in-thread \`hello there\` follow-up triggers an outbound encrypted-message burst (Olm session prepares for the typing indicator + final reply) in the parents room. No \`skipping room message\` entries fire for the in-thread events:

  ```
  \"time\":\"2026-05-22T05:43:08.376-05:00\" \"matrix: logged in as @openclaw:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net\"
  \"time\":\"2026-05-22T05:44:08.376-05:00\" \"[MatrixClient.[!pgXEKemZcQTfeiaNvM:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net encryption]] m1779446648376.1: Checking for missing Olm sessions\"
  \"time\":\"2026-05-22T05:44:08.464-05:00\" \"[MatrixClient.[!pgXEKemZcQTfeiaNvM:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net encryption]] m1779446648464.2: Checking for missing Olm sessions\"
  \"time\":\"2026-05-22T05:44:08.537-05:00\" \"[MatrixClient.[!pgXEKemZcQTfeiaNvM:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net encryption]] m1779446648537.3: Checking for missing Olm sessions\"
  \"time\":\"2026-05-22T05:44:08.614-05:00\" \"[MatrixClient.[!pgXEKemZcQTfeiaNvM:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net encryption]] m1779446648614.4: Checking for missing Olm sessions\"
   (+ 7 more bursts of streaming-quiet typing updates ending at m1779446649228.11)
  ```

  11 outbound encrypted-message preparations in the same thread within 853 ms — the streaming-quiet typing-indicator pattern fires throughout the agent turn, then a final reply. Compare to the pre-patch deployment captured earlier in #85111's reproduction, where the same in-thread message produced a `\"message\":\"skipping room message\"` entry at `handler.ts:1032` with no outbound activity.

  Runtime acceptance suite (verbose) against `308da8c5` — the three behaviors this PR is responsible for:

  ```
   ✓ monitor/handler > createMatrixRoomMessageHandler > bypasses requireMention for in-thread replies when a session record already exists for the thread
   ✓ monitor/handler > createMatrixRoomMessageHandler > bypasses requireMention for in-thread replies when an explicit runtime binding exists (e.g. /focus)
   ✓ monitor/handler > createMatrixRoomMessageHandler > still drops in-thread replies without mention when bypassMentionInBoundThreads is off (default)
     (+ 107 other existing handler behaviors, all green)

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  110 passed (110)
     Duration  2.92s
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: On the live patched deployment, an in-thread `hello there` (no `@openclaw` mention) inside the thread the bot just opened triggers the agent — Element shows `OpenClaw is typing…`, the user's message gets the `👀` ack reaction, the bot replies inside the same thread. Pre-patch, the identical message was logged as `skipping room message` and never reached the agent. Top-level non-mention messages in the same room continue to be dropped (legacy `requireMention` behavior preserved). With the new flag off (the default for everyone who doesn't opt in), behavior is byte-identical to current `main`, and the existing regression at `handler.test.ts:1617` (`does not refresh bound Matrix thread bindings for room messages dropped before routing`) continues to pass without modification.

- **What was not tested**: The unmodified-image runtime (i.e. with `bypassMentionInBoundThreads: true` set in `openclaw.json` and a CI-rebuilt `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:latest` carrying the patch through the official build pipeline). The live proof above uses an in-place bundle overlay on a running container, which exercises the patched mention-gate code path correctly but uses the always-on form of the discriminator (the running runtime has no way to read the new opt-in config field). The flag-gated form is what the source PR ships; the gate evaluation is covered by the three handler tests above, two of which exercise the bypass path and one of which exercises the legacy-default-off path. Maintainer-side CI rebuild will produce the final runtime artifact that exercises the flag-gated form end-to-end.

## Discriminator update vs the originally-pushed version

The first commit on this branch (`6f2a5b6e`) gated the bypass on `_runtimeBindingId !== null` only. Live testing surfaced that natural per-thread continuation does NOT produce a runtime binding — `sessionBindingService.resolveByConversation` returns `null` for threads that were opened by the bot replying, only for threads that have an explicit ACP/user-managed binding (via `/focus`, `/acp bind`, etc.). The handler tests in `6f2a5b6e` mocked `resolveByConversation` to return a binding, which masked the wrong discriminator: tests passed because the mock matched the broken code path, not because the real-world code path worked.

The current commit (`308da8c5`) replaces the single-signal check with an OR over two signals:

1. **Session-store record exists for the thread** — via `core.channel.session.readSessionUpdatedAt({ storePath, sessionKey: _route.sessionKey })`. The thread session key is set by `resolveMatrixInboundRoute` whenever `thread.threadId` is populated (always under `threadReplies: \"inbound\"`/`\"always\"`), and `recordInboundSession` persists it on the first message the bot processes in the thread. So this is the natural-continuation path.
2. **Explicit runtime binding exists** — the original `_runtimeBindingId !== null` check, kept as a parallel signal for the `/focus`-bound case.

The session-store path is the dominant case in real deployments; the runtime-binding path covers explicit-binding flows for parity. Either signal is sufficient.

## Files changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `extensions/matrix/src/config-schema.ts` | New `bypassMentionInBoundThreads` field in `matrixThreadBindingsSchema` |
| `extensions/matrix/src/types.ts` | Same field added to `MatrixThreadBindingsConfig` TS type |
| `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/index.ts` | Read `accountConfig.threadBindings?.bypassMentionInBoundThreads === true` and pass into handler params |
| `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/handler.ts` | Handler params accept the flag; in-thread-reply discriminator computes session-store existence via `readSessionUpdatedAt`; OR with `_runtimeBindingId` and feeds `shouldBypassMention` |
| `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/handler.test.ts` | Three tests: session-record bypass, runtime-binding bypass, legacy default-off drop |
| `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/handler.test-helpers.ts` | Test harness option `bypassMentionInBoundThreads` |
| `docs/channels/matrix.md` | Documents the new field plus discriminators and the preserved legacy contract |

## Related

- #85017 / #85018 — DM classification veto on `is_direct: false` (companion PR; same `#parents`/`#adults` deployment thread).
- #85111 — the filed issue with full reproduction.

target.title: fix(cron): guard against undefined sourceDelivery in isolated executor
target.body:
## Summary

Guard against `undefined` `sourceDelivery` in the isolated cron executor to prevent `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')`.

## Problem

Commit `4c613fbfe0` ("refactor(cron): centralize source delivery plan", 2026-05-18) replaced three separate params (`messageChannel`, `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, `toolPolicy`) with a unified `sourceDelivery: SourceDeliveryPlan` across the lazy-import boundary between `run.ts` and `run-executor.ts`.

A runtime version skew (stale compiled JS from a partial build or ESM module cache) causes `params.sourceDelivery` to be `undefined` for isolated cron jobs, crashing at `run-executor.ts:147`.

## Fix

1. Made `sourceDelivery` optional in both `createCronPromptExecutor` and `executeCronRun`
2. When `sourceDelivery` is undefined, reconstruct a `SourceDeliveryPlan` that **preserves legacy caller fields**:
   - Reads `toolPolicy` from stale caller params (`disableMessageTool`, `forceMessageTool`, `requireExplicitMessageTarget`)
   - Reads `sourceReplyDeliveryMode` from stale caller params
   - Reads `messageChannel` from stale caller params
   - Falls back to `resolvedDelivery` for channel/to/accountId/threadId
3. Added `logWarn` diagnostic to flag the fallback

This ensures webhook callers that disabled the message tool still have it disabled, announce callers that set `message_tool_only` keep that mode, and no security boundary is widened.

## Tests

5 test cases in `run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts`:
1. `sourceDelivery: undefined` (no legacy fields) → reconstructs with safe defaults ✅
2. `sourceDelivery: undefined` → falls back to `resolvedDelivery` channel/to/accountId/threadId ✅
3. Valid `sourceDelivery` → works normally (regression) ✅
4. **Stale announce caller**: `sourceDelivery: undefined` + `toolPolicy: { disableMessageTool: false, forceMessageTool: true }` + `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"` → preserves those values ✅
5. **Stale webhook caller**: `sourceDelivery: undefined` + `toolPolicy: { disableMessageTool: true, forceMessageTool: false }` → message tool stays disabled ✅

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: All `sessionTarget: "isolated"` cron jobs crash with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')`
- **Real environment tested**: Docker containers built from OpenClaw source, Linux aarch64 (OrbStack), Node.js v24.16.0

### Failing proof (upstream/main, before fix)

Built Docker image from upstream/main and ran `executeCronRun` from compiled `dist/` with `sourceDelivery: undefined`:

```
$ docker run --rm openclaw-main-proof "node scripts/cron-proof.mjs"

=== Issue #85051 Real Behavior Proof ===
Environment: linux arm64 Node v24.16.0

Loading: run-executor.runtime-CmqBcLXN.js
Test: executeCronRun with sourceDelivery=undefined

❌ CRASH (sourceDelivery bug): TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')
   This is the exact bug reported in issue #85051
TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')
    at createCronPromptExecutor (file:///app/dist/run-executor.runtime-CmqBcLXN.js:81:56)
    at executeCronRun (file:///app/dist/run-executor.runtime-CmqBcLXN.js:237:19)
```

### Passing proof (fix branch, after fix)

```
$ docker run --rm openclaw-cron-fix-v4 "node scripts/cron-proof.mjs"

=== Issue #85051 Real Behavior Proof ===
Environment: linux arm64 Node v24.16.0

Loading: run-executor.runtime-Cl47DXeI.js
Test: executeCronRun with sourceDelivery=undefined

[cron:proof-job] sourceDelivery is undefined; using fallback — possible build artifact mismatch
✅ PASSED sourceDelivery guard
   (subsequent error is expected without LLM config: All models failed...)
```

### Docker vitest proof (all 5 tests including legacy caller shapes)

```
$ docker run --rm openclaw-cron-fix-v4 "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts"

✓ cron ../../src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts (5 tests) 47ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  5 passed (5)
  Duration  843ms
```

Fixes #85051

## Summary

- Clarify the Codex native-assets doctor warning so users install the external Codex plugin before running `openclaw migrate codex --dry-run` when the migration provider is unavailable.
- Update the doctor CLI docs to match the warning.
- Cover the revised warning text in the existing Codex native-assets doctor test.

## Tests

- RED: `node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-native-assets.test.ts --run` (failed before implementation with the old warning text)
- GREEN: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-native-assets.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Doctor warns users about personal Codex CLI assets and now tells them to install the external Codex plugin first if needed before running `openclaw migrate codex --dry-run`.

Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout on this PR head with existing node_modules, using an isolated temporary HOME, OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR, OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH, and CODEX_HOME. The source checkout had no built `dist/entry.mjs`, so I invoked the CLI `runCli` entry directly with argv `doctor --non-interactive`, exercising the same doctor command path that renders OpenClaw doctor output.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a temporary config with `plugins.entries.codex.enabled=true` and `agents.defaults.agentRuntime.id="codex"`, created a real personal skill at `$HOME/.agents/skills/agent-helper/SKILL.md`, then ran:

```sh
HOME="$ROOT/home" CODEX_HOME="$ROOT/home/.codex" OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$ROOT/state" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$ROOT/home/.openclaw/openclaw.json" OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node --import tsx --input-type=module -e 'import { runCli } from "./src/cli/run-main.ts"; await runCli(["node","openclaw","doctor","--non-interactive"]);'
```

Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal output excerpt from the real doctor CLI run:

```text
◇  Doctor warnings ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                                          │
│  - Personal Codex CLI assets were found, but native Codex-mode OpenClaw  │
│    agents use isolated per-agent Codex homes.                            │
│  - Sources: [temp-home]/.codex and [temp-home]/.agents/skills (1 skill,  │
│    0 plugins, 0 config files, 0 hook files).                             │
│  - These assets will not be loaded by the Codex app-server child unless  │
│    you intentionally promote them.                                       │
│  - If the Codex plugin is not installed, run `openclaw plugins install   │
│    npm:@openclaw/codex` first. Then run `openclaw migrate codex          │
│    --dry-run` to inventory them. Applying that migration copies skills   │
│    into the current OpenClaw agent workspace; Codex plugins, hooks, and  │
│    config stay manual-review only.                                       │
│                                                                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

Observed result after fix: The after-patch doctor output displayed the revised Codex plugin prerequisite warning before `openclaw migrate codex --dry-run`.

What was not tested: A globally installed built `openclaw` binary was not used because this checkout lacked `dist/entry.mjs`; full `pnpm check:changed` and `pnpm docs:list` were not rerun locally because CI already completed the changed-file checks on this PR and previous local pnpm invocations attempted dependency reconciliation with optional package downloads.

target.title: fix(acp): add distributed session locking with fail-closed redis fallback
target.body:
## Summary
Adds distributed ACP session locking with Redis support and fail-closed behavior when Redis is configured but unavailable.

## What changed
- added `SessionLockManager` abstraction
- added `LocalSessionLockManager`
- added `RedisSessionLockManager`
- added `FailClosedSessionLockManager`
- wired lock acquire/release/renew into `tryDispatchAcpReply`
- skip ACP dispatch with `acp_execution_locked` when lock is contended or acquire fails
- documented:
  - `OPENCLAW_ACP_SESSION_LOCK_REDIS_URL`
  - `OPENCLAW_ACP_SESSION_LOCK_TTL_MS`
- added unit + dispatch integration tests

## Locking behavior
- no Redis configured → local in-process lock manager
- Redis configured and healthy → Redis lock manager
- Redis configured but init fails → fail closed, no fallback to local
- release only if owner matches
- renew only if owner matches
- renew is best-effort during long-running turns

## Validation
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `pnpm tsgo`
- `pnpm vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/session-lock-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts`

target.title: feat: write restart sentinel on CLI restart to notify user after gate…
target.body:
…way restart

When running 'openclaw gateway restart' via CLI, the gateway would previously not notify the user after restarting. This fix writes a restart sentinel after confirming restart actually occurred, allowing the gateway to send a notification message to the user once it comes back up.

The sentinel mechanism already exists for agent-initiated restarts (gateway tool), but CLI restarts were missing this feature.

**Bug fix (v2):** Moved sentinel write to after `runServiceRestart` returns. Only write sentinel when result === true (restart actually occurred). This prevents false 'restart succeeded' notifications when gateway was not running.

target.title: Gateway: add webchat internal-run regression coverage
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: #46106 reported that webchat clients could miss agent replies resumed by `subagent_announce` / inter-session turns after `sessions_yield`.
- Why it matters: when that path regresses, webchat looks hung even though the reply is present in the session transcript.
- What changed: added focused regression coverage in `src/gateway/server.agent.gateway-server-agent-b.test.ts` for internal webchat-visible agent turns in both the main-session and direct-session paths.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no runtime gateway logic, transport contracts, or frontend code changed in this PR.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens

- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #46106

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None on current `main`. This PR locks in the expected webchat delivery behavior from #46106 with direct regression tests.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 + pnpm + Vitest
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): webchat agent event streaming
- Relevant config (redacted): test harness defaults

### Steps

1. Start the gateway websocket test harness for agent/webchat event streaming.
2. Register a webchat-visible run context and emit internal `agent` turns without going through `agent.wait`.
3. Assert that webchat observers still receive `agent_turn_complete` for resumed/internal runs.

### Expected

- Webchat observers receive internal-run agent turn events for both the resumed main-session path and the direct webchat session path.

### Actual

- Current `main` already behaves correctly in these scenarios; the new tests pass and preserve that behavior as regression coverage.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Issue evidence and logs are in #46106. This PR adds direct automated coverage for the same delivery path.

Verification commands run locally:

- `pnpm test -- src/gateway/server.agent.gateway-server-agent-b.test.ts -t 'agent events stream to webchat clients when run context is registered'`
- `pnpm test -- src/gateway/server.agent.gateway-server-agent-b.test.ts -t 'webchat observers receive'`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/gateway/server.agent.gateway-server-agent-b.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/gateway/server.agent.gateway-server-agent-b.test.ts`

Note: `pnpm test -- src/gateway/server.agent.gateway-server-agent-b.test.ts` still hits an unrelated existing failure in `agent accepts channel aliases (imsg/teams)` locally; the focused scenarios above pass.

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: ran the existing webchat run-context stream test plus the two new internal-turn regression tests.
- Edge cases checked: resumed parent-session delivery and direct webchat-session delivery.
- What you did **not** verify: a manual browser repro against a live webchat frontend.

target.title: feat(session): add chat-channel session switching with LRU history retention
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Chat channels did not support listing or switching between prior sessions under the same SessionKey, and follow-up fixes were needed to preserve switchable transcripts until LRU eviction and restore per-session usage/compaction snapshots correctly.
- Why it matters: Without in-chat session switching, users cannot navigate past conversations from Feishu/DingTalk/Telegram/Discord-style chat surfaces; incorrect transcript/archive or snapshot behavior would also make `/session back`, `/sessions`, and `/status`-adjacent state feel unreliable.
- What changed: Added `/sessions`, `/session <number>`, `/session <sessionId>`, and `/session back` for chat channels; introduced per-SessionKey LRU `sessionHistory`; preserved switchable transcripts until history eviction; restored token/context/compaction snapshots on switch; documented matching/order/retention behavior; cleaned up reset semantics so `session.historyLimit=0` truly disables switchable history.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not change SessionKey routing, does not add cross-SessionKey switching, does not add named sessions/UI picker flows, and does not change channel plugin-specific transport behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #9959
- Related #28168

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).  
If none, write `None`.

- Added `/sessions` to list switchable sessions for the current chat SessionKey.
- Added `/session <number>`, `/session <sessionId>`, and `/session back` for in-chat session switching.
- Current session is included in `/sessions`, marked as `[current]`.
- `/sessions` now shows compact single-line preview rows with session ID prefix and truncated preview text.
- Session switching now restores stored token/context/compaction snapshots for the target session.
- `session.historyLimit` now consistently supports `0` as “disable switchable session history”.
- Transcripts for sessions still in history remain switchable; transcripts are archived when evicted from the LRU history queue.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local OpenClaw dev runtime / source tree
- Model/provider: N/A for core session-switch logic
- Integration/channel (if any): chat-channel command flow; manually validated in a real chat session
- Relevant config (redacted):
  - `session.historyLimit: 5` (default path)
  - `session.historyLimit: 0` (disable-history regression path)

### Steps

1. Start a chat conversation and create multiple sessions with `/new`.
2. Run `/sessions`, then switch via `/session <number>` or `/session back`.
3. Continue creating sessions until the history limit is exceeded.

### Expected

- `/sessions` lists current + recent switchable sessions in LRU order.
- Switching restores the target session’s stored metadata/snapshots.
- Sessions still present in history remain switchable.
- The oldest session is archived only after LRU eviction.
- With `session.historyLimit=0`, switchable history is disabled and the previous session is archived immediately on reset.

### Actual

- Behavior matches the expected results above in targeted tests and manual validation.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `/sessions` output in chat
  - `/session back` restores the previous session
  - switching to a still-retained historical session restores expected context/state
  - LRU eviction archives only the oldest evicted session
  - daily reset path preserves switchable history
  - `session.historyLimit=0` disables switchable history and archives immediately on reset
- Edge cases checked:
  - history boundary behavior at the configured limit
  - selecting current session is a no-op
  - unique-prefix SessionId selection behavior
  - stale/daily-reset session rollover
- What you did **not** verify:
  - every individual chat provider/plugin surface
  - cross-SessionKey switching (out of scope)
  - any UI/card-based session picker (out of scope)

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

target.title: fix: preserve logical symlinked session paths
target.body:
## Summary
- keep realpath-based safety checks for session files, but return and persist logical session paths under the caller-visible sessions directory
- normalize legacy stored real paths back to the logical symlinked path so `openclaw doctor` does not report false orphan transcripts
- add regression coverage for symlinked session dirs, sibling fallback handling, and doctor orphan detection

## Reproduction
Before this change, a symlinked `~/.openclaw` caused `resolveSessionFilePath()` to canonicalize `sessionFile` values into the symlink target. `openclaw doctor` scans the logical sessions directory, so those stored real paths were misclassified as orphaned transcripts.

## Validation
- reproduced the mismatch with a temporary symlinked state dir before the fix
- reran the same harness after the fix and verified both logical and legacy stored real paths resolve back to the logical transcript path
- added regression coverage in `src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts` and `src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.test.ts`

## AI Assistance
- AI-assisted: yes (implemented with Codex plus manual verification and follow-up fixes)
- Testing degree: targeted/source-level validation plus focused regression tests in the changed modules; full local `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` was not available in the initial sandbox, so broader validation is currently delegated to repository CI

Fixes #49708
